meaning_zh: 做一件事同时达到两个目的。
meaning_en: To kill two birds with one stone.
example: 小明去社区图书馆还书，顺便又借了几本新书，一箭双雕，真划算。
example_en: Xiao Ming went to the community library to return his books and borrowed several new ones at the same time — truly killing two birds with one stone.

meaning_zh: 做多余的事，反而不恰当。
meaning_en: To gild the lily; to ruin something by adding something superfluous.
example: 你的作文已经写得很好了，再加这句话反而画蛇添足。
example_en: Your composition is already very well written — adding that extra sentence would only gild the lily.

meaning_zh: 犯错后及时补救，还不算晚。
meaning_en: It's never too late to mend.
example: 虽然他这次考试没考好，但只要亡羊补牢，努力学习，下次一定能进步。
example_en: Even though he did not do well in this exam, it is never too late to mend — as long as he studies hard, he will certainly improve next time.

meaning_zh: 坏事有时能变成好事。
meaning_en: A blessing in disguise.
example: 小明丢了钱包，心里很难过，但塞翁失马，焉知非福——第二天他在组屋走廊捡到了更多零用钱。
example_en: Xiao Ming was upset about losing his wallet, but every cloud has a silver lining — the next day he found even more pocket money near the corridor of his HDB block.

meaning_zh: 违反规律，急于求成，反而坏事。
meaning_en: To spoil things by excessive enthusiasm.
example: 妈妈每天逼着妹妹做很多练习题，这不是爱她，而是拔苗助长。
example_en: Making his sister do countless practice papers every day is not love — it is forcing her growth and will only be counterproductive.

meaning_zh: 眼光狭窄，见识短浅的人。
meaning_en: A person with a very narrow outlook.
example: 我们要多读书、多旅行，不然就会变成井底之蛙。
example_en: We must read widely and explore the world; otherwise we will end up with a very narrow outlook.

meaning_zh: 形容骚扰得很厉害，连鸡狗都不得安宁。
meaning_en: Great disorder or confusion.
example: 弟弟在家里大吵大闹，弄得全家鸡犬不宁。
example_en: Younger brother was making such a racket at home that the whole family was thrown into complete chaos.

meaning_zh: 言行前后抵触，互不相容。
meaning_en: To contradict oneself.
example: 你刚才说喜欢红色，现在又说不喜欢，这不是自相矛盾吗？
example_en: You said you liked red just now, and now you are saying you do not — aren't you contradicting yourself?

meaning_zh: 下定决心，不顾一切干到底。
meaning_en: To burn one's bridges; to cut off all means of retreat.
example: 为了这次考试，我决定破釜沉舟，专心复习。
example_en: For this exam, I have decided to give it everything I have and focus entirely on revision.

meaning_zh: 疑神疑鬼，自相惊扰。
meaning_en: Extremely suspicious; imaginary fears.
example: 他因为上次被骗，现在看到陌生电话就杯弓蛇影，不敢接。
example_en: After being scammed once, he now jumps at every unfamiliar phone call, gripped by imaginary fears.

meaning_zh: 形容书法笔力强劲，也比喻见解深刻。
meaning_en: Written in a forceful hand; penetrating; profound.
example: 老师对这篇文章的分析入木三分，让我学到了很多。
example_en: The teacher's analysis of the essay was penetrating and insightful — I learnt a great deal from it.

meaning_zh: 倚仗别人的威势来欺压人。
meaning_en: To bully people by flaunting one's powerful connections.
example: 他仗着自己是班长就欺负同学，真是狐假虎威。
example_en: He bullied his classmates by flaunting his position — a classic case of borrowing another's authority to throw his weight around.

meaning_zh: 做事之前已经有全面的考虑和把握。
meaning_en: To have a well-thought-out plan; to have confidence.
example: 他复习得很充分，所以考试时胸有成竹。
example_en: He had revised thoroughly, so he sat the exam with complete confidence.

meaning_zh: 以为自己不知道，别人也不知道。
meaning_en: To deceive oneself; to bury one's head in the sand.
example: 他上课偷吃零食，以为老师没看见，其实是掩耳盗铃。
example_en: He was eating snacks in class and thought the teacher had not noticed — he was only deceiving himself.

meaning_zh: 在关键处加上一两句，使内容更加生动有力。
meaning_en: To add the finishing touch.
example: 文章的结尾加上这一句，起到了画龙点睛的作用。
example_en: Adding that final sentence to the composition gave it just the right finishing touch.

meaning_zh: 事情的真相完全显露出来。
meaning_en: The truth comes out; everything is revealed.
example: 经过警察叔叔的调查，这件事终于水落石出了。
example_en: After the investigation by the police officer, the truth of the matter was finally brought to light.

meaning_zh: 得到非常投合心意的人或环境。
meaning_en: Like a duck to water; in one's element.
example: 小玲第一次参加学校游泳课，一跳进泳池就游得十分自在，简直如鱼得水。
example_en: The moment Xiao Ling jumped into the pool during her first school swimming lesson, she took to it like a duck to water.

meaning_zh: 从一件事情类推，知道许多事情。
meaning_en: To learn by analogy; to draw inferences.
example: 老师教我们学习要会举一反三，这样才能真正掌握知识。
example_en: The teacher taught us to learn by analogy, so that we can truly master what we study.

meaning_zh: 学生超过老师，后人胜过前人。
meaning_en: The student surpasses the master.
example: 华文老师指出，小明写的作文比他去年的水平高出许多，真是青出于蓝。
example_en: The Chinese language teacher pointed out that Xiao Ming's compositions had surpassed even his own level from last year — truly a case of the student surpassing the master.

meaning_zh: 不忘本，懂得感恩。
meaning_en: To remember one's roots; to be grateful.
example: 我们要饮水思源，感谢父母的养育之恩。
example_en: We must always remember where we came from and be grateful to our parents for raising us.

meaning_zh: 事情进行到中途，想停止却又无法停止。
meaning_en: To have no way to back down; to be in a dilemma.
example: 比赛已经报名了，不去参加又不好，真是骑虎难下。
example_en: He had already signed up for the competition, and pulling out would be awkward — he was well and truly caught in a dilemma.

meaning_zh: 无关紧要的小事或毫无价值的东西。
meaning_en: Trivial matters; insignificant things.
example: 好朋友之间不要为了一些鸡毛蒜皮的小事吵架。
example_en: Good friends should not fall out over such trivial and insignificant matters.

meaning_zh: 坏的到了尽头，好的就来了。
meaning_en: After a storm comes a calm; every cloud has a silver lining.
example: 别灰心，我们耐心等待，总会否极泰来的。
example_en: Do not lose heart — if we wait patiently, things will surely turn around.

meaning_zh: 孤立无援，四面受敌的处境。
meaning_en: To be besieged on all sides; to be in dire straits.
example: 他因为说谎，朋友们都不理他了，现在的他真是四面楚歌。
example_en: Because he had lied, all his friends stopped talking to him — he now found himself completely isolated.

meaning_zh: 一个人力量薄弱，难以成事。
meaning_en: It's hard to achieve anything without support.
example: 想做好这件事，需要大家一起努力，我一个人孤掌难鸣。
example_en: To do this well, everyone must work together — one person alone cannot accomplish it.

meaning_zh: 事物周密完善，没有破绽。
meaning_en: Flawless; without a flaw.
example: 这两个人的谎话编得天衣无缝，但还是被警察识破了。
example_en: The two of them had woven a seamless web of lies, yet the police still saw through them.

meaning_zh: 在别人急需时给予帮助。
meaning_en: To offer timely help; a friend in need is a friend indeed.
example: 在我生病的时候，同学们来看我，真是雪中送炭。
example_en: When I was ill, my classmates came to visit me — it was just the help I needed at the hardest moment.

meaning_zh: 极大的数量中的极小一部分。
meaning_en: A drop in the bucket; very small portion.
example: 对于全校数百名同学来说，我这次为班级赢得的一枚奖牌只是九牛一毛，但我已尽力了。
example_en: Among the hundreds of pupils in our school, the one medal I won for the class is just a drop in the ocean — but I gave my best.

meaning_zh: 死守狭隘经验，不知变通。
meaning_en: To wait for a windfall; to stick to old habits.
example: 学习要主动，不能守株待兔，等着老师把知识送上门。
example_en: Learning must be active — you cannot simply wait by the tree stump for the teacher to deliver knowledge to your doorstep.

meaning_zh: 看势头或看别人眼色行事。
meaning_en: To trim one's sails; to be opportunistic.
example: 他这个人很会见风使舵，老师说什么他就跟着说什么。
example_en: He always shifts with the wind — whatever the teacher says, he immediately agrees with.

meaning_zh: 形容事情顺利，一做就成。
meaning_en: To gain an immediate victory; to succeed immediately.
example: 祝你这次考试马到成功，考出好成绩！
example_en: Wishing you every success in this exam!

pinyin: shèng bù jiāo, bài bù něi
meaning_zh: 成功了不骄傲，失败了不气馁。
meaning_en: Not to get cocky when winning, nor lose heart when losing.
example: 我们要有胜不骄、败不馁的精神，才能不断进步。
example_en: We must have the spirit of staying humble in victory and resilient in defeat, so that we can keep improving.

meaning_zh: 使美好的事物更美好。
meaning_en: To add beauty to beauty; to make what is good even better.
example: 他已经考了第一名，你又给他买礼物，真是锦上添花。
example_en: He had already come first in class, and now you are buying him a gift on top of that — adding flowers to brocade.

meaning_zh: 顺手拿走人家的东西。
meaning_en: To walk away with something; to pick up something on the sly.
example: 他看到同桌的橡皮很漂亮，就顺手牵羊拿走了，这是不对的。
example_en: He spotted his deskmate's attractive eraser and casually took it away — that was simply wrong.

meaning_zh: 惩罚一个来警告其余的人。
meaning_en: To punish someone as a warning to others.
example: 老师批评了小力，是想杀鸡儆猴，让其他同学安静下来。
example_en: The teacher singled out Xiao Li for a reprimand as a warning to the rest of the class to settle down.

meaning_zh: 仪表或才能在周围一群人里显得很突出。
meaning_en: To stand out from the crowd; to be head and shoulders above others.
example: 小美的个子很高，在人群里就像鹤立鸡群一样。
example_en: Xiao Mei is very tall, and she stands out in a crowd just like a crane among chickens.

meaning_zh: 危害集体的人。
meaning_en: A black sheep; a person who brings disgrace upon the group.
example: 我们要团结友爱，不做班级里的害群之马。
example_en: We must be united and caring, and never be the bad apple that spoils the class.

meaning_zh: 对不懂道理的人讲道理。
meaning_en: To cast pearls before swine.
example: 他跟完全不懂足球的人讲足球，简直就是对牛弹琴。
example_en: Explaining the offside rule to someone who has never watched football is like playing music to a buffalo.

meaning_zh: 不自量力，自取灭亡。
meaning_en: To fight a hopeless battle; to court defeat.
example: 我们班跟校篮球队比赛，那是以卵击石，肯定会输。
example_en: Our class playing against the school basketball team would be like an egg hitting a rock — we would surely lose.

meaning_zh: 力量太小，解决不了问题。
meaning_en: An utterly inadequate measure; a drop in the bucket.
example: 他欠了很多钱，我借给他这点钱只是杯水车薪。
example_en: He owed a great deal of money, and the small amount I lent him was barely a drop in the bucket.

meaning_zh: 形容人官运亨通或因成功而得意。
meaning_en: To be immensely proud of one's success; to be in high spirits.
example: 期中成绩放榜，小明考了全班第一，这几天春风得意，走路都轻快了许多。
example_en: After the mid-year results were released and Xiao Ming came top of the class, he has been walking on air these past few days.

meaning_zh: 用空想来安慰自己。
meaning_en: To feed on illusions; to console oneself with fantasy.
example: 他没有努力学习，却想考好成绩，那只是画饼充饥。
example_en: Hoping to score well without putting in any effort is nothing but wishful thinking.

meaning_zh: 空谈理论，不能解决实际问题。
meaning_en: To be an armchair strategist; to engage in idle theorizing.
example: 光说不练，那是纸上谈兵，我们要动手去做。
example_en: All talk and no action is mere armchair strategising — we must get down to actually doing it.

meaning_zh: 只要坚持不懈，事情就能成功。
meaning_en: Constant dripping wears away the stone; perseverance prevails.
example: 只要我们有水滴石穿的精神，就一定能学会英语。
example_en: Learning a language requires the perseverance of water wearing through stone.

meaning_zh: 坏人互相勾结干坏事。
meaning_en: To conspire together; to work hand in glove with.
example: 这两个人狼狈为奸，一起偷了别人的钱包。
example_en: The two of them were in cahoots and stole someone's wallet together.

meaning_zh: 过于追求速度，反而达不到目的。
meaning_en: Haste makes waste; more haste, less speed.
example: 写作业不能只求快，否则错误很多，这就是欲速则不达。
example_en: You cannot rush through your homework just to finish quickly — making lots of mistakes proves that haste makes waste.

meaning_zh: 行动不谨慎，惊动了对方。
meaning_en: To alert the enemy by premature actions.
example: 警察叔叔说，先不要抓他，以免打草惊蛇，抓不到大坏人。
example_en: The police officer advised us not to approach him yet, in case we scared off the bigger culprits.

meaning_zh: 坏人在走投无路时，会不顾一切地行动。
meaning_en: A cornered beast will do something desperate.
example: 被警察包围后，那个小偷狗急跳墙，想从窗户逃走。
example_en: When surrounded by the police, the thief made a desperate bid to escape through the window.

meaning_zh: 受着这一方的好处，暗地里却为另一方尽力。
meaning_en: To live off one person while secretly helping another; to be a traitor.
example: 他竟然把小组作业的想法告诉了对手班级，让对方提前准备，真是吃里扒外。
example_en: He actually revealed the group project ideas to the rival class so they could prepare in advance — a complete act of betrayal.

meaning_zh: 地位、名声、价值等迅速往上升。
meaning_en: To soar rapidly; on the rise.
example: 这位同学加入辩论队后，表现扶摇直上，连老师们都对他刮目相看。
example_en: Since joining the debate team, that pupil's performance has soared, and even the teachers have taken notice.

meaning_zh: 假装出强大的气势来吓唬人。
meaning_en: To make a deceptive show of power; to bluff.
example: 他故意大声说话，其实是在虚张声势，他也很害怕。
example_en: He was speaking loudly on purpose, but it was all bluster — he was just as frightened as everyone else.

meaning_zh: 情况万分危急。
meaning_en: In a critical situation; hanging by a thread.
example: 就在千钧一发之际，消防员叔叔接住了从楼上掉下来的小孩。
example_en: At the very last critical moment, the firefighter managed to catch the child who had fallen from the upper floor.

meaning_zh: 对事物只凭片面的了解就下结论。
meaning_en: To take a part for the whole; to be unable to see the big picture.
example: 我们看问题要全面，不能像盲人摸象一样，只看一部分。
example_en: We must look at issues from all angles and not be like blind men touching an elephant, seeing only a part of the whole.

meaning_zh: 射击准确，比喻做事有充分把握。
meaning_en: To be a crack shot; to be absolutely certain.
example: 他是个神枪手，每次打靶都百发百中。
example_en: He is a crack shot — every single attempt hits the mark.

pinyin: yú yǔ xióng zhǎng bù kě jiān dé
meaning_zh: 两个好事不能同时拥有，必须做出选择。
meaning_en: You can't have it both ways.
example: 你想考高分，又不想努力学习，可是鱼与熊掌不可兼得啊。
example_en: You want top grades but are unwilling to study hard — you simply cannot have your cake and eat it too.

meaning_zh: 经验丰富的人熟悉情况，能起引导作用。
meaning_en: An old hand knows the ropes; experience counts.
example: 爷爷在这一带住了几十年，对附近的组屋区了如指掌，真是老马识途，带着我们很快就找到了那家诊所。
example_en: Grandfather has lived in this neighbourhood for decades and knows every corner like the back of his hand — with him leading the way, we found the clinic in no time.

meaning_zh: 比喻心肠狠毒，忘恩负义。
meaning_en: Rapacious and cruel; ungrateful.
example: 他帮助了那个孩子，那孩子却偷了他的东西，真是狼心狗肺。
example_en: He helped that child, only for the child to steal from him afterwards — what a heartless and ungrateful act.

meaning_zh: 快乐得不想回家了。
meaning_en: To indulge in pleasure and forget home and duty.
example: 小明在嘉年华会上玩得不亦乐乎，简直乐不思蜀，忘记了回家的时间。
example_en: Xiao Ming was having such a wonderful time at the funfair that he completely lost track of time and forgot to head home.

meaning_zh: 失势之后，重新恢复地位或力量。
meaning_en: To stage a comeback; to return to power.
example: 虽然这次比赛输了，但他没有灰心，准备明年东山再起。
example_en: Even though he lost this competition, he did not lose heart and is already preparing for a comeback next year.

meaning_zh: 假装同情，假慈悲。
meaning_en: To shed crocodile tears.
example: 明明是他欺负我，现在又来安慰我，真是猫哭老鼠。
example_en: He was the one who bullied me, and now he is coming to comfort me — the ultimate act of crocodile tears.

pinyin: qiān lǐ zhī dī huǐ yú yǐ xué
meaning_zh: 小事不注意，就会酿成大祸。
meaning_en: A small leak will sink a great ship; slight negligence may lead to great disaster.
example: 我们要注意安全，不能忽视小问题，因为千里之堤毁于蚁穴。
example_en: We must never overlook small problems, because a mighty dam can collapse from a tiny crack in the wall.

meaning_zh: 没有十全十美的事物。
meaning_en: There are spots even on the sun; no one is perfect.
example: 每个人都有缺点，金无足赤，人无完人嘛。
example_en: Everyone has their flaws — no gold is completely pure, and no person is perfect.

pinyin: tuì yī bù hǎi kuò tiān kōng
meaning_zh: 对别人宽容一点，自己的天地也会更宽广。
meaning_en: To give way a little and you will see a broader view; to be tolerant.
example: 同学之间吵架，只要退一步海阔天空，就还是好朋友。
example_en: When classmates quarrel, all it takes is one step back and a broader view — you will still be good friends.

pinyin: lóng shēng lóng, fèng shēng fèng
meaning_zh: 指有什么样的父母，就有什么样的孩子。
meaning_en: Like father, like son; what's bred in the bone will come out in the flesh.
example: 小伟的爸爸是厨师，他也从小爱下厨，假期里常常在家捣鼓各种菜式，真是龙生龙，凤生凤。
example_en: Xiao Wei's father is a chef, and he has loved cooking since young, often experimenting with different dishes at home during the holidays — a true case of like father, like son.

meaning_zh: 能力差的人，做事要先走一步。
meaning_en: The slow need to start early; diligence compensates for slowness.
example: 我脑子慢，只能笨鸟先飞，多花些时间来学习。
example_en: My mind works slowly, so I have to be the early bird — putting in more time and effort than others to keep up.

meaning_zh: 按照线索寻找，也比喻做事死板。
meaning_en: To look for something according to a plan; to be rigid in one's approach.
example: 我们按照说明书，按图索骥，终于把模型拼好了。
example_en: Following the instruction manual step by step, we finally managed to assemble the model successfully.

pinyin: yù bàng xiāng zhēng, yú wēng dé lì
meaning_zh: 两方争执，会让第三方得利。
meaning_en: If two parties fight, a third party will benefit.
example: 你们两个别吵了，再吵下去，只会鹬蚌相争，渔翁得利。
example_en: Stop arguing, the two of you — if you keep this up, a third party will walk away with all the gains.

meaning_zh: 旧的已经用完，新的还未接上。
meaning_en: Temporary shortage; a gap in the sequence.
example: 家里的水果吃完了，新的还没买，正是青黄不接的时候。
example_en: The fruit at home had all been eaten and the new supply had not yet been bought — right in the middle of a temporary shortage.

meaning_zh: 一个人得势，和他有关系的人也跟着发迹。
meaning_en: When a man gets to the top, all his friends and relations get there with him.
example: 班长当选了学生领袖，他的几个好朋友也跟着鸡犬升天，纷纷获得了小组组长的职位。
example_en: When the class monitor was elected a student leader, a few of his close friends also rode on his coattails and became group leaders.

meaning_zh: 格格不入，不能相合。
meaning_en: To be incompatible; a square peg in a round hole.
example: 他们两个的性格就像方枘圆凿，很难成为朋友。
example_en: The two of them are completely incompatible in character — like a square peg in a round hole — and find it hard to get along.

meaning_zh: 极难找到。
meaning_en: To look for a needle in a haystack.
example: 在全岛数以万计的学生中找到那个遗失钱包的主人，简直是大海捞针。
example_en: Trying to find the owner of a lost wallet among tens of thousands of pupils island-wide is like looking for a needle in a haystack.

meaning_zh: 说话很有分量，能起决定作用。
meaning_en: One's word carries weight; to be as good as one's word.
example: 爸爸做事公正，在邻里互助小组里说话一言九鼎，大家都很敬重他。
example_en: Father is fair and dependable, and his word carries great weight in the neighbourhood residents' committee.

meaning_zh: 说话直截了当，切中要害。
meaning_en: To hit the nail on the head; to touch one's sore spot.
example: 老师一针见血地指出了我作文中的缺点。
example_en: The teacher pinpointed the weaknesses in my composition with one incisive remark.

meaning_zh: 对别人的危难不去帮助，在一旁看热闹。
meaning_en: To look on at others' troubles with indifference; to be a bystander.
example: 同学遇到困难，我们要帮助他，不能隔岸观火。
example_en: When a classmate is in difficulty, we must help — we cannot simply stand on the sidelines and watch.

pinyin: zhòng guā dé guā, zhòng dòu dé dòu
meaning_zh: 有什么样的因，就有什么样的果。
meaning_en: As you sow, so shall you reap.
example: 只要你努力学习，就能取得好成绩，这就是种瓜得瓜，种豆得豆。
example_en: As long as you study hard, you will reap good results — you truly reap what you sow.

meaning_zh: 把坏人招引进来。
meaning_en: To open the door to a dangerous foe; to bring disaster upon oneself.
example: 他让那个陌生人住在家里，没想到是引狼入室，家里的东西被偷了。
example_en: He let that stranger stay in his home, not realising he was inviting trouble inside — and things went missing shortly after.

meaning_zh: 嘴上说得好听，心里却打着害人的主意。
meaning_en: To be honey-mouthed and dagger-hearted; hypocritical and malicious.
example: 他是个口蜜腹剑的人，你要小心他。
example_en: He is the sort of person who is all smiles to your face but harbours ill intentions — do be careful of him.

meaning_zh: 表面上爱好某事物，实际上并不真懂。
meaning_en: To profess a love of what one really fears; to be a hypocritical enthusiast.
example: 他嘴上说喜欢运动，可从来不锻炼，真是叶公好龙。
example_en: He keeps saying he loves exercise, but never actually works out — a classic case of professing to love something he does not.

meaning_zh: 消息和传说不是完全没有根据的。
meaning_en: Not entirely without foundation; an empty hole invites the wind.
example: 这件事是有根据的，不是空穴来风。
example_en: This matter is well-founded and is by no means a groundless rumour.

meaning_zh: 不如退而结网。
meaning_en: Better to go back and make a net than to stand by the pond and long for fish.
example: 与其临渊羡鱼，不如退而结网，自己动手去做。
example_en: Rather than standing at the edge wishing for fish, it is better to go and weave a net — take action and do it yourself.

meaning_zh: 运用智慧，借力打力。
meaning_en: To achieve one's purpose by using the enemy's strength.
example: 老师给我们讲了草船借箭的故事，让我们明白智慧往往比蛮力更重要。
example_en: The teacher told us the story of the Straw Boat Borrowing Arrows, showing us that wisdom will always triumph over brute force.

meaning_zh: 真相是掩盖不住的。
meaning_en: Truth will out; murder will out.
example: 他做错了事想隐瞒，但纸包不住火，最后还是被发现了。
example_en: He tried to hide his mistake, but the truth cannot be concealed forever — it was discovered in the end.

meaning_zh: 达到目的后，就把帮助过自己的人踢开。
meaning_en: To kick down the ladder; to be ungrateful.
example: 他利用完朋友就把人家忘了，这种过河拆桥的行为很不好。
example_en: He made use of his friend and then forgot all about him — that kind of burn-your-bridges behaviour is truly unacceptable.

meaning_zh: 变化多端，反复无常。
meaning_en: To blow hot and cold; to be fickle.
example: 他今天喜欢画画，明天喜欢唱歌，总是朝三暮四的。
example_en: Today he likes drawing, tomorrow he likes singing — he is always chopping and changing.

meaning_zh: 故意颠倒黑白，混淆是非。
meaning_en: To deliberately misrepresent; to call black white.
example: 他为了讨好老师，竟然指鹿为马，硬说是小明先讲话，真是太不诚实了。
example_en: To curry favour with the teacher, he deliberately twisted the truth and said it was Xiao Ming who had been talking — that was deeply dishonest.

pinyin: shù yù jìng ér fēng bù zhǐ
meaning_zh: 客观情况不以人的意志为转移。
meaning_en: Things don't always go the way you want; circumstances are beyond one's control.
example: 我想安静地在房间里温习功课，楼下走廊却传来孩子们玩耍的嬉闹声，真是树欲静而风不止。
example_en: I wanted to revise quietly in my room, but the laughter and play of children drifting up from the corridor downstairs made it impossible.

pinyin: bān qǐ shí tou zá zì jǐ de jiǎo
meaning_zh: 本想害人，结果害了自己。
meaning_en: To hoist oneself with one's own petard.
example: 他想骗别人，结果反而被骗了，真是搬起石头砸自己的脚。
example_en: He set out to trick someone else, but ended up being tricked himself — he brought it entirely upon himself.

meaning_zh: 劳而无功，白费力气。
meaning_en: To achieve nothing; to waste one's energy.
example: 不努力就想成功，就像竹篮打水一场空。
example_en: Hoping to succeed without putting in any effort is as futile as carrying water in a bamboo basket — everything will come to nothing.

meaning_zh: 秘密商量事情时，要防被偷听。
meaning_en: Beware of eavesdroppers.
example: 在组屋走廊里说话要小心，隔墙有耳，不要随便议论别人。
example_en: Keep your voice down — the walls have ears, and we do not want others to overhear our plans.

pinyin: chuán dào qiáo tóu zì rán zhí
meaning_zh: 事情到了最后，总会有解决的办法。
meaning_en: Things will work out in the end; in the end, things will mend.
example: 别担心，车到山前必有路，船到桥头自然直。
example_en: Do not worry — things have a way of working themselves out when the time comes.

pinyin: bīng dòng sān chǐ, fēi yī rì zhī hán
meaning_zh: 事情的形成，都有一个长期积累的过程。
meaning_en: Rome wasn't built in a day.
example: 他的英语这么好，是因为长期努力的结果，冰冻三尺非一日之寒。
example_en: His English is so strong because it is the result of years of sustained effort — Rome was not built in a day.

meaning_zh: 去做根本做不到的事。
meaning_en: To chase an illusion; to make impractical or vain efforts.
example: 不学习就想考大学，就像水中捞月一样，根本不可能。
example_en: Expecting to do well without studying is as impossible as trying to fish the moon out of the water.

pinyin: yuǎn shuǐ bù jiù jìn huǒ
meaning_zh: 缓慢的解决办法，满足不了紧急的需要。
meaning_en: A slow remedy cannot meet an urgent need.
example: 幸好楼上邻居阿姨及时发现，送奶奶去附近诊所，否则等亲戚从外地赶来，早就远水不救近火了。
example_en: Fortunately, the neighbour upstairs noticed in time and helped Grandmother to the nearby clinic — otherwise, waiting for relatives to come from afar would have been a case of too little, too late.

meaning_zh: 事到临头才想办法。
meaning_en: To act too late; not to make timely preparations.
example: 平时不努力，考试前才临渴掘井，肯定来不及。
example_en: Doing nothing all term and only scrambling to revise the night before the exam is like digging a well when you are already thirsty — it is simply too late.

meaning_zh: 不自量力，必然失败。
meaning_en: To overrate oneself and try to hold back an overwhelmingly superior force.
example: 他一个人想跟整个班级对抗，简直是螳臂当车。
example_en: One person trying to stand against the entire class is like a mantis trying to stop a chariot — completely futile.

meaning_zh: 一个人的力量有限，做不成大事。
meaning_en: One person alone cannot save the situation.
example: 虽然他很努力，但整个项目太大，他一个人独木难支。
example_en: Although he worked very hard, the project was simply too large for one person to hold up alone.

meaning_zh: 时间拖长了，事情可能会发生不利的变化。
meaning_en: A long delay means many hitches; delays are dangerous.
example: 我们尽快把这件事定下来吧，免得夜长梦多。
example_en: Let us settle this matter as soon as possible, in case unexpected complications arise the longer we wait.

meaning_zh: 刻苦自励，发愤图强。
meaning_en: To endure hardships to achieve some ambition.
example: 为了实现梦想，他卧薪尝胆，刻苦训练了很多年。
example_en: Driven by his dream, he endured years of gruelling training, putting up with every hardship along the way.

meaning_zh: 粗略地观察一下，不深入。
meaning_en: To gain a shallow understanding from a fleeting glance.
example: 去博物馆要仔细看，不能走马观花，那样学不到东西。
example_en: When visiting the museum, you must look carefully — rushing through without stopping to take things in means you will come away having learnt nothing.

pinyin: shuǐ néng zài zhōu, yì néng fù zhōu
meaning_zh: 百姓能拥护君王，也能推翻他。
meaning_en: The people can support or overthrow their ruler; double-edged sword.
example: 老师告诉我们，要尊重同学，因为水能载舟，亦能覆舟。
example_en: The teacher reminded us that just as water can carry a boat or capsize it, our actions towards others can make or break relationships.

meaning_zh: 专心致志，没有别的念头。
meaning_en: Heart and soul; with undivided attention.
example: 上课时，我们要一心一意地听老师讲课。
example_en: During lessons, we must give our undivided attention to what the teacher is saying.

meaning_zh: 靠自己的力量把事情做好。
meaning_en: Self-reliance; to stand on one's own feet.
example: 哥哥考上了理工学院，坚持不向家里要零用钱，选择自力更生，课余时间在食阁打工。
example_en: Even though his family is well-off, Elder Brother chose to be self-reliant and took on a part-time job at the food court to pay for his polytechnic fees.

meaning_zh: 把帮助别人当作快乐。
meaning_en: To take pleasure in helping others.
example: 看到马路边的老婆婆提着重重的购物袋，志明毫不犹豫地上前帮忙，真是助人为乐。
example_en: Seeing an elderly lady struggling with heavy shopping bags by the roadside, Zhi Ming stepped forward without hesitation to help — a true example of finding joy in helping others.

meaning_zh: 做一件事，得到两种好处。
meaning_en: To kill two birds with one stone.
example: 放学后，小丽顺道帮奶奶在组屋楼下的超市买菜，再一起回家，真是一举两得。
example_en: After school, Xiao Li stopped by the supermarket on the ground floor of their HDB block to buy groceries for Grandmother on her way home — truly killing two birds with one stone.

meaning_zh: 事情做到一半就停止了，不能坚持。
meaning_en: To give up halfway; to leave something unfinished.
example: 做任何事情都不能半途而废，要坚持到底。
example_en: Whatever you set out to do, you must never give up halfway — always see it through to the end.

meaning_zh: 把全部力量都投进去。
meaning_en: To go all out; to spare no effort.
example: 运动会就要到了，我们要全力以赴，争取拿到好成绩。
example_en: Sports Day is just around the corner — we must give it our all and strive for a good result.

meaning_zh: 大家说的都一样。
meaning_en: To speak with one voice; all agreeing.
example: 同学们异口同声地回答了老师的问题。
example_en: The pupils answered the teacher's question in one voice.

meaning_zh: 喜欢得不肯放手。
meaning_en: To be so fond of something that one will not let it out of one's hand.
example: 妹妹在书展上买到一套心仪已久的漫画书，爱不释手，连吃饭都捧着看。
example_en: Younger sister received that set of comics at the Book Fair and has been utterly captivated by it — she even brought it to bed with her.

meaning_zh: 注意力高度集中。
meaning_en: To concentrate one's attention; to be absorbed in.
example: 华文课上，同学们聚精会神地听老师讲解作文技巧，教室里鸦雀无声。
example_en: During the Chinese language lesson, the pupils listened to the teacher's composition tips with rapt attention, and the classroom fell completely silent.

meaning_zh: 一下子就明白过来了。
meaning_en: Suddenly to see the light; to realize something all of a sudden.
example: 经过老师的讲解，我恍然大悟，原来这道题这么简单。
example_en: After the teacher's explanation, I suddenly had a flash of understanding — the question was actually so straightforward!

meaning_zh: 没有察觉到，没有意识到。
meaning_en: Unconsciously; before one knows it.
example: 做完所有的家庭作业，不知不觉已经是晚上九点了。
example_en: Time flies — before I knew it, I had finished all my homework and it was already nine o'clock at night.

meaning_zh: 吃得很有味道，或对某事很有兴趣。
meaning_en: To do something with great gusto; to eat with appetite.
example: 小息时间，阿明坐在食堂的角落里，津津有味地翻看着图书馆借来的故事书。
example_en: During recess, Ah Ming sat in the corner of the canteen, thoroughly absorbed in a storybook he had borrowed from the library.

meaning_zh: 急得不能再等待了。
meaning_en: Unable to hold oneself back; to be impatient.
example: 放学铃声一响，小明迫不及待地冲出课室，跑到篮球场和同学们打球。
example_en: The moment the bell rang, Xiao Ming dashed out of the classroom and could not wait to get to the basketball court.

meaning_zh: 人多口杂，议论纷纷。
meaning_en: All talking at once; hubbub of voices.
example: 小息时间，同学们在食堂里七嘴八舌地讨论着校庆表演该穿什么服装。
example_en: During recess, the pupils were all talking over one another in the canteen, debating what costumes to wear for the school anniversary performance.

meaning_zh: 非常安静。
meaning_en: Dead silence; absolute quietness.
example: 老师一进教室，全班立刻鸦雀无声。
example_en: The moment the teacher walked into the classroom, the whole class fell pin-drop silent.

meaning_zh: 兴致高，情绪热烈。
meaning_en: Full of joy; in great delight.
example: 学校庆祝国庆节，同学们兴高采烈地在走廊布置红白气球和彩旗。
example_en: To celebrate National Day, the pupils were in high spirits as they decorated the corridor with red and white balloons and bunting.

meaning_zh: 形容失望懊丧的样子。
meaning_en: Downcast; dejected; crestfallen.
example: 小华因为数学测验没考好，垂头丧气地拿着卷子走出课室。
example_en: Xiao Hua did not do well in his Mathematics test and walked out of the classroom with his head hanging in dejection.

meaning_zh: 因意想不到的好事而特别高兴。
meaning_en: To be overjoyed; to be pleasantly surprised.
example: 妈妈答应周末带我去新加坡动物园，我喜出望外，立刻把功课都做完了。
example_en: Mum promised to bring me to the Singapore Zoo at the weekend, and I was over the moon — I finished all my homework on the spot.

meaning_zh: 形容非常害怕。
meaning_en: To tremble with fear; to be scared stiff.
example: 站在滨海湾金沙酒店的空中花园向下俯视，让人心惊胆战，双腿发软。
example_en: Looking straight down from the Sky Park at Marina Bay Sands made everyone's heart race and their legs turn to jelly.

meaning_zh: 非常生气的样子。
meaning_en: Furiously angry; in a towering rage.
example: 小峰发现自己的文具盒被人动过，怒气冲冲地走到班长跟前询问。
example_en: Xiao Feng discovered that someone had been through his pencil case and marched over to the class monitor in a furious temper.

meaning_zh: 心情不好，不高兴。
meaning_en: To feel depressed; to be in low spirits.
example: 小丽不知道为什么和好朋友闹翻了，一整天都闷闷不乐，提不起劲来。
example_en: Xiao Li did not know why she had fallen out with her best friend, and spent the whole day feeling low and listless.

meaning_zh: 哭得很伤心的样子。
meaning_en: Cheeks bathed in tears.
example: 校庆表演结束，看到老师和同学们台上台下拥抱道别，她感动得泪流满面。
example_en: When the school anniversary performance ended and teachers and pupils were embracing and saying their goodbyes on stage and off, she was moved to tears.

meaning_zh: 高兴到了极点。
meaning_en: To be wild with joy; to go into raptures.
example: 听到新加坡队在东南亚运动会夺得金牌，球迷们欣喜若狂，在小贩中心里相互击掌庆祝。
example_en: When news broke that the Singapore team had won gold at the Southeast Asian Games, fans were absolutely overjoyed, high-fiving one another at the hawker centre.

meaning_zh: 胆子非常小。
meaning_en: As timid as a mouse; chicken-hearted.
example: 弟弟胆小如鼠，连邻居养的小狗跑过来也吓得躲到妈妈身后。
example_en: Younger brother is as timid as a mouse — even the neighbour's little dog running over sends him cowering behind Mum.

meaning_zh: 心神极为不安。
meaning_en: Nervous and uneasy; fidgety.
example: 成绩单快要派发了，我心里忐忑不安，不知道自己这次的华文科考得如何。
example_en: The report cards were about to be handed out, and I was on tenterhooks, wondering how I had done in Chinese.

meaning_zh: 非常高兴。
meaning_en: To burst with joy; to be overjoyed.
example: 老师宣布我们班获得了合唱节的金奖，全班同学顿时心花怒放，欢呼雀跃。
example_en: When the teacher announced that our class had won the gold award at the Choral Festival, everyone's heart bloomed with joy and they burst into cheers.

meaning_zh: 忧愁痛苦的样子。
meaning_en: To have a worried look; to pull a long face.
example: 别整天愁眉苦脸的，PSLE还有几个月，好好复习还来得及。
example_en: Stop moping around with that long face — the PSLE is still a few months away, and there is plenty of time to revise properly.

meaning_zh: 非常害怕，脸色都变了。
meaning_en: To be so frightened that one's face turns pale.
example: 小明打碎了学校走廊的花盆，看到老师走过来，吓得大惊失色，脸色发白。
example_en: Xiao Ming had knocked over a flower pot in the school corridor, and when he saw the teacher approaching, he went pale with fright.

meaning_zh: 充满了欢喜的神色或气氛。
meaning_en: Full of joy; jubilant.
example: 农历新年到了，组屋里家家户户张灯结彩，一片喜气洋洋的景象。
example_en: Chinese New Year had arrived, and every household in the HDB block was decorated with festive lights and ornaments, creating an atmosphere of joy all around.

meaning_zh: 愤怒得头发竖起，顶起帽子。
meaning_en: To bristle with anger; to be in a towering rage.
example: 小文得知好友背着他向老师说谎，得知真相后气得怒发冲冠。
example_en: Xiao Wen discovered that his friend had lied to the teacher behind his back, and was absolutely furious when he learnt the truth.

meaning_zh: 坚持到底，一点不松懈。
meaning_en: To persist in; to stick to something without letup.
example: 只要我们坚持不懈地努力，就一定能成功。
example_en: As long as we persevere in our efforts without giving up, we will surely succeed.

meaning_zh: 无论受多少挫折都不退缩。
meaning_en: To be undaunted by repeated setbacks; to be indomitable.
example: 在科学探索的道路上，需要百折不挠的精神。
example_en: The road of scientific discovery requires a spirit of indomitable perseverance in the face of repeated setbacks.

meaning_zh: 雕刻一件东西，一直刻下去不放手。
meaning_en: To work with perseverance; to keep on chipping away.
example: 他锲而不舍地练习书法，终于写出了漂亮的字。
example_en: He kept at his calligraphy practice with unwavering persistence, and eventually produced beautifully formed characters.

meaning_zh: 工作或学习勤奋不知疲倦。
meaning_en: To work hard and untiringly; to be diligent.
example: 老师孜孜不倦地教导我们，我们很感激他。
example_en: The teacher guided us with tireless dedication, and we are deeply grateful for everything she has done.

meaning_zh: 非常专心，顾不上睡觉和吃饭。
meaning_en: To be so absorbed as to forget food and sleep.
example: 为了完成这个模型，他简直是废寝忘食。
example_en: He was so absorbed in completing the model that he practically forgot to eat and sleep.

meaning_zh: 勤奋能弥补天生的笨拙。
meaning_en: Diligence is the means by which one makes up for one's dullness.
example: 我虽然脑子慢，但我相信勤能补拙。
example_en: Even though I am a slow learner, I firmly believe that hard work and diligence can make up for a lack of natural talent.

meaning_zh: 只要坚持不懈，事情就能成功。
meaning_en: Constant effort brings success.
example: 学习外语要有水滴石穿的精神。
example_en: Learning a foreign language requires the spirit of water wearing through stone — slow, steady, and unrelenting.

meaning_zh: 只要有毅力，再难的事也能做成。
meaning_en: Little strokes fell great oaks.
example: 只要有铁杵磨针的决心，再难的题目也能做出来。
example_en: With the determination to grind an iron pestle into a needle, even the hardest problem can be solved.

meaning_zh: 长久地坚持下去。
meaning_en: To persevere; in a persistent way.
example: 锻炼身体要持之以恒，不能三天打鱼两天晒网。
example_en: Exercise must be kept up consistently — you cannot exercise for three days and rest for two.

meaning_zh: 在困难面前不屈服。
meaning_en: Unbending in face of difficulties.
example: 我们要学习科学家不屈不挠的探索精神。
example_en: We should learn from the scientist's spirit of unyielding exploration in the face of every obstacle.

meaning_zh: 振作精神，以求强盛。
meaning_en: To rise in great vigor; to make a determined effort to become strong.
example: 少年强则国强，我们要好好学习，奋发图强。
example_en: If the youth of a nation are strong, the nation will be strong — we must study hard and strive to be our best.

meaning_zh: 很能吃苦，不怕劳累。
meaning_en: To bear hardships and work hard.
example: 华人的传统中，刻苦耐劳历来是一种受人尊敬的美德，我们应该继承这种精神。
example_en: In Chinese tradition, being hardworking and persevering through difficulties has always been a virtue worthy of respect — a spirit we should carry forward.

meaning_zh: 下定决心，努力谋求强盛。
meaning_en: To work hard for the prosperity of one's country.
example: 虽然小峰英语基础较差，但他发愤图强，每天提早半小时到校向老师请教。
example_en: Although Xiao Feng had a weaker foundation in English, he worked with fierce determination, arriving at school half an hour early every day to seek guidance from the teacher.

meaning_zh: 日夜不停。
meaning_en: Day and night; round the clock.
example: 为了在校庆表演前完成道具制作，同学们夜以继日地在美术室赶工。
example_en: To complete the props in time for the school anniversary performance, the pupils worked day and night in the art room to meet the deadline.

meaning_zh: 受到别人的恩德，要想着回报。
meaning_en: To be grateful and seek to repay the kindness.
example: 是林老师在我成绩最差的时候一直鼓励我，我要知恩图报，努力学习，不辜负她的心血。
example_en: It was Teacher Lim who kept encouraging me when my results were at their lowest — I must repay her kindness by studying hard and not letting her effort go to waste.

meaning_zh: 看到正义的事，就勇敢地去做。
meaning_en: To act bravely for a just cause.
example: 邻居王叔叔看到组屋走廊有人昏倒，立即上前施救，这种见义勇为的精神值得我们学习。
example_en: Neighbour Uncle Wang saw someone collapse in the HDB corridor and rushed over to help immediately — his courageous act of compassion is worth emulating.

meaning_zh: 喜欢做善事，乐于施舍。
meaning_en: To love to do charitable deeds; to be philanthropic.
example: 社区关怀角的张阿姨乐善好施，每逢节日都会亲手煮饭菜，分送给独居老人。
example_en: Auntie Zhang from the community care corner is generous and kind-hearted — every festive season, she cooks home-made food to share with elderly residents who live alone.

meaning_zh: 言行一致，遵守诺言。
meaning_en: To be honest and keep one's word.
example: 我们要做一个诚实守信的好孩子。
example_en: We must be honest and keep our word — these are the qualities of a good person.

meaning_zh: 虚心，不自满，小心谨慎。
meaning_en: Modest and cautious.
example: 即使取得了好成绩，我们也要保持谦虚谨慎的态度。
example_en: Even after achieving good results, we must remain humble and not become complacent.

meaning_zh: 尊敬长辈，爱护晚辈。
meaning_en: To respect the aged and cherish the young.
example: 尊老爱幼是我们社会的核心价值之一，乘坐地铁时，我们应主动为老人和小孩让座。
example_en: Respecting the elderly and caring for the young is one of the core values of our society — on the MRT, we should always offer our seats to the elderly and children.

meaning_zh: 捡到财物不据为己有。
meaning_en: To return lost property to its owner.
example: 小明在组屋电梯间拾到一个钱包，毫不犹豫地交给了楼层的保安，这种拾金不昧的精神值得表扬。
example_en: Xiao Ming found a wallet outside the lift lobby of his HDB block and handed it straight to the security guard — his honesty in returning lost property deserves recognition.

meaning_zh: 文雅有礼貌的样子。
meaning_en: Refined and courteous; urbane.
example: 新来的转校生小杰对人总是彬彬有礼，很快就赢得了同学和老师的喜爱。
example_en: The new transfer pupil, Xiao Jie, was always polite and courteous to everyone, and quickly won the affection of both classmates and teachers.

meaning_zh: 心地光明，胸怀坦白。
meaning_en: Open and aboveboard; straightforward.
example: 班长处理同学纠纷时总是光明磊落，从不偏袒任何一方，大家都信服他。
example_en: When handling disputes between classmates, the class monitor was always open and fair, never taking sides — and everyone respected him for it.

meaning_zh: 做官廉洁，没有贪污。
meaning_en: To be an upright and honest official.
example: 社区关怀角的义工叔叔服务多年，从未收取分文报酬，真正做到两袖清风。
example_en: The volunteer uncle at the community care corner has served for many years and never accepted a single cent in return — a man of true integrity.

meaning_zh: 用自己的行动做出榜样。
meaning_en: To set an example by one's own actions.
example: 老师要求我们不迟到，他自己先做到，这就是以身作则。
example_en: The teacher asks us never to be late, and he himself arrives first every morning — that is leading by example.

meaning_zh: 为了他人而牺牲自己的利益。
meaning_en: To sacrifice one's own interests for the sake of others.
example: 运动会接力赛中，小伟扭伤了脚踝，仍坚持把棒交给下一棒，舍己为人，令全场感动。
example_en: During the relay race on Sports Day, Xiao Wei twisted his ankle but pressed on to pass the baton to the next runner — his selfless sacrifice moved everyone at the event.

meaning_zh: 完全为人民群众着想，毫无私心。
meaning_en: Selfless; perfectly impartial.
example: 老师分配小组任务时，大公无私，从不因为谁是朋友而特别照顾。
example_en: When assigning group tasks, the teacher was completely impartial and never gave preferential treatment to any pupil just because of personal ties.

meaning_zh: 对自己要求严格。
meaning_en: To be strict with oneself.
example: 作为班级风纪股长，小慧严以律己，从不在走廊奔跑，也从不迟到。
example_en: As the class discipline prefect, Xiao Hui held herself to the highest standards — she never ran in the corridors and was never once late for school.

meaning_zh: 对别人宽容。
meaning_en: To be generous and lenient towards others.
example: 我们要学会严以律己，宽以待人。
example_en: We should learn to be strict with ourselves and lenient with others.

meaning_zh: 学习没有尽头。
meaning_en: There is no limit to knowledge.
example: 虽然我已经考了第一名，但我知道学无止境，还要继续努力。
example_en: Even though I came first in class, I know that learning has no end — there is always more to strive for.

meaning_zh: 复习旧知识，能获得新理解。
meaning_en: To gain new insights through restudying old material.
example: 老师说，温故知新，是一种很好的学习方法。
example_en: The teacher told us that reviewing what we have learnt and discovering new insights is one of the most effective study methods.

meaning_zh: 谦虚好学，向不如自己的人请教。
meaning_en: To not feel ashamed to ask one's subordinates or people below.
example: 小慧在班上成绩名列前茅，但遇到不懂的数学题，仍会虚心向同学请教，真是不耻下问。
example_en: Although Xiao Hui is consistently among the top pupils in class, she is never too proud to ask a classmate when she encounters something she does not understand.

meaning_zh: 广泛地阅读各种书。
meaning_en: To be well-read.
example: 他之所以知识渊博，是因为从小就博览群书。
example_en: The reason he is so knowledgeable is that he has been reading widely ever since he was young.

meaning_zh: 按照一定的步骤逐渐深入或提高。
meaning_en: To follow in order and advance step by step.
example: 学习是一个循序渐进的过程，不能急于求成。
example_en: Learning is a step-by-step process — you cannot try to leap ahead before the foundations are solid.

meaning_zh: 吸取别人的长处，弥补自己的短处。
meaning_en: To overcome one's shortcomings by learning from others' strong points.
example: 同学之间要互相学习，取长补短，共同进步。
example_en: Classmates should learn from one another's strengths and make up for each other's weaknesses, progressing together.

meaning_zh: 灵活地学习和运用知识。
meaning_en: To apply creatively and flexibly what one has learned.
example: 学习成语不能死记硬背，要活学活用。
example_en: Learning idioms should not be about rote memorisation — we must understand them deeply and apply them flexibly in our writing.

meaning_zh: 一心一意，集中精神。
meaning_en: To be wholly absorbed; to devote one's mind to.
example: 弟弟在专心致志地搭积木，谁叫他也不理。
example_en: Younger brother was so focused on building his block tower that he paid no attention to anyone who called his name.

meaning_zh: 勤奋学习，喜欢提问。
meaning_en: To be diligent and fond of asking questions.
example: 他是一个勤学好问的好学生，老师很喜欢他。
example_en: He is a diligent and inquisitive student who is always eager to learn — the teachers are very fond of him.

meaning_zh: 把多方面的知识贯穿起来，得到透彻的理解。
meaning_en: To achieve a thorough understanding of the subject through mastery of all aspects.
example: 对学过的知识，要学会融会贯通，不能死记硬背。
example_en: Knowledge that has been learnt must be thoroughly understood and integrated — rote memorisation alone will not serve you well.

meaning_zh: 掌握了某一事物的知识，就能推知同类事物。
meaning_en: To comprehend by analogy; to draw inferences.
example: 数学老师教我们举一反三，触类旁通，这样难题就都难不倒我们了。
example_en: The Mathematics teacher taught us to think by analogy and draw inferences — that way, even the most difficult problems could not stump us.

meaning_zh: 追究底细，盘问清楚。
meaning_en: To investigate the root of the matter; to get to the bottom of.
example: 他是个好奇心很强的孩子，什么事都要刨根问底。
example_en: He is an intensely curious child who always wants to get to the bottom of everything.

meaning_zh: 勤奋好学，不肯放下书本。
meaning_en: To be very studious; to read voraciously.
example: 爷爷虽然年纪大了，但依然手不释卷，天天看书。
example_en: Even in his old age, Grandfather still reads every single day, never once putting down a book.

meaning_zh: 为了实际应用而学习。
meaning_en: To study for the purpose of application; to put what one learns into practice.
example: 我们学习知识要学以致用，不能只学不用。
example_en: We must put what we learn into practice — knowledge that is never applied is knowledge wasted.

meaning_zh: 争着向前，唯恐落后。
meaning_en: To vie with each other in doing something.
example: 书展开幕那天，同学们争先恐后地涌向儿童读物区，挑选自己喜欢的书。
example_en: On the opening day of the Book Fair, pupils rushed eagerly to the children's section, jostling to pick out their favourite titles.

meaning_zh: 自己跟自己说话。
meaning_en: To soliloquize; to think aloud.
example: 爷爷年纪大了，有时看到旧照片，会一个人自言自语地回忆往事。
example_en: Grandfather is getting on in years, and sometimes sits quietly looking at old photographs, murmuring to himself as he recalls the past.

meaning_zh: 举动十分谨慎，丝毫不敢疏忽。
meaning_en: Cautiously; very carefully.
example: 小明小心翼翼地端着一碗热汤从厨房走向餐桌，生怕洒在地板上。
example_en: Xiao Ming carried a bowl of hot soup from the kitchen to the dining table with the utmost care, terrified of spilling a single drop.

meaning_zh: 做事认真，一点儿不马虎。
meaning_en: Meticulously attentive; to be scrupulous about every detail.
example: 老师批改作文时一丝不苟，连标点符号都逐一检查，从不马虎。
example_en: When marking compositions, the teacher is painstakingly thorough — checking every single punctuation mark without fail.

meaning_zh: 东张西望，不专心的样子。
meaning_en: To look around; to peep left and right.
example: 考试时要专心作答，不能左顾右盼，否则会被误会作弊。
example_en: During an exam, you must keep your eyes on your own paper and not look around — otherwise you risk being suspected of cheating.

meaning_zh: 做事慌乱，没有条理。
meaning_en: In a flurry; to act with confusion.
example: 小明忘记今天要交读书报告，才手忙脚乱地翻遍书包，找出昨晚写的稿子。
example_en: Xiao Ming had forgotten that his book report was due today, and scrambled frantically through his bag to dig out the draft he had written the night before.

meaning_zh: 眼珠一动不动地盯着看。
meaning_en: To look fixedly; to gaze intently.
example: 弟弟目不转睛地盯着电视里的机器人动画，连妈妈叫他吃饭都没听见。
example_en: Younger brother stared unblinkingly at the robot cartoon on television, completely oblivious to Mum calling him for dinner.

meaning_zh: 吃东西又猛又急的样子。
meaning_en: To gobble up; to devour ravenously.
example: 踢完球回来，小峰饿坏了，盛了一大碗饭，狼吞虎咽地吃了起来。
example_en: After football training, Xiao Feng was ravenous — he piled his plate high and wolfed it all down in no time.

meaning_zh: 出汗很多的样子，形容非常累或着急。
meaning_en: To be sweating all over; to be in a sweat.
example: 体育课跑完两圈操场，同学们个个满头大汗，纷纷跑去水壶架喝水。
example_en: After running two laps of the sports field during Physical Education, every pupil was dripping with sweat and rushed to the water bottle rack for a drink.

meaning_zh: 非常高兴的样子。
meaning_en: To beam with joy; to be all smiles.
example: 老师在全班面前表扬小丽的作文写得生动，她立刻眉开眼笑，高兴得合不拢嘴。
example_en: When the teacher praised Xiao Li's composition in front of the whole class, she immediately broke into a broad, beaming smile.

meaning_zh: 高兴得失去了常态。
meaning_en: To get dizzy with success; to be on cloud nine.
example: 小华期中考试考了班上第二名，便得意忘形，上课开始分心，结果期末成绩反而退步了。
example_en: Xiao Hua came second in class for the mid-year exams and grew so pleased with himself that he stopped paying attention in lessons — and his results actually dropped by the year-end.

meaning_zh: 说不出话来，形容理屈或害怕。
meaning_en: To be at a loss for words; to gape with astonishment.
example: 老师突然叫他回答问题，他完全没有留意，被问得张口结舌，一句话也答不出来。
example_en: The teacher called on him out of the blue to answer a question he had completely missed, and he stood there tongue-tied, unable to utter a single word.

meaning_zh: 充分显示自己的本领。
meaning_en: To give a good account of oneself; to show one's mettle.
example: 学校运动会上，小伟在跑步项目中大显身手，为班级夺得了一枚银牌。
example_en: At the school Sports Day, Xiao Wei really showed what he was made of in the running events and won a silver medal for the class.

meaning_zh: 到处看，形容注意力不集中。
meaning_en: To gaze around; to peer in all directions.
example: 过马路时要专心看交通灯，不能东张西望，以免发生意外。
example_en: When crossing the road, you must stay focused and not look around absent-mindedly, or you risk an accident.

meaning_zh: 天气晴朗暖和。
meaning_en: Bright sun and gentle breeze; fine weather.
example: 今天风和日丽，是个放风筝的好天气。
example_en: The day was bright and breezy — perfect weather for flying a kite.

meaning_zh: 色彩非常繁多。
meaning_en: Colorful; of various colors.
example: 花园里开满了五颜六色的鲜花。
example_en: The garden was in full bloom, splashed with flowers of every colour.

meaning_zh: 形容百花齐放，色彩艳丽。
meaning_en: A riot of color; a blaze of color.
example: 春天到了，公园里万紫千红，美丽极了。
example_en: Spring had arrived, and the park was a riot of colour — truly a beautiful sight.

meaning_zh: 春天美好的景象。
meaning_en: Birds sing and flowers bloom; a beautiful spring scene.
example: 春天的校园鸟语花香，像一幅画。
example_en: The school grounds were alive with birdsong and the fragrance of blossoms — like a picture-perfect scene.

meaning_zh: 风景优美。
meaning_en: Picturesque scenery; beautiful landscape.
example: 我的家乡是个山清水秀的好地方。
example_en: My hometown is a place of picturesque hills and clear streams.

meaning_zh: 一眼望不到边，非常辽阔。
meaning_en: Stretching beyond the horizon; boundless.
example: 站在东海岸公园的堤岸上眺望，大海一望无际，海天相接，令人心旷神怡。
example_en: Standing on the embankment at East Coast Park and gazing out to sea, the ocean stretched endlessly to the horizon, where sky and water merged into one — a breathtaking sight.

meaning_zh: 树木枝叶茂密，遮住了阳光。
meaning_en: To be shaded by green trees.
example: 街道两旁绿树成荫，走在上面很舒服。
example_en: The road was lined on both sides with tall, leafy trees casting a cool, dense shade — a pleasure to walk beneath.

meaning_zh: 各种瓜果成熟时飘散的香味。
meaning_en: The air is filled with the aroma of fruits.
example: 秋天到了，果园里瓜果飘香。
example_en: The harvest season had arrived, and the orchard was filled with the sweet fragrance of ripe fruits.

meaning_zh: 风雨及时，适合农时。
meaning_en: Favorable weather; good climatic conditions.
example: 今年风调雨顺，粮食获得了大丰收。
example_en: This year the weather has been favourable, and crops have yielded an abundant harvest.

meaning_zh: 春天气候温暖，百花盛开。
meaning_en: During the warmth of spring all the flowers bloom.
example: 等到了春暖花开的季节，我们一起去郊游。
example_en: When the warm spring season arrives and flowers are in full bloom, let us go on an outing together.

meaning_zh: 秋天天空晴朗，气候凉爽。
meaning_en: The autumn sky is clear and the air is crisp.
example: 秋高气爽，正是登高的好时节。
example_en: The crisp, clear autumn weather is the perfect time for hiking and enjoying the outdoors.

meaning_zh: 冰雪漫天盖地，非常寒冷。
meaning_en: A world of ice and snow; very cold.
example: 在科学课上，老师播放了极地冰天雪地的影片，同学们看得目瞪口呆。
example_en: During a science lesson, the teacher showed a documentary about the frozen, snow-covered polar regions, and the pupils watched in wide-eyed amazement.

meaning_zh: 凶猛而使人害怕的波涛。
meaning_en: Tempestuous storms; a situation or life full of perils.
example: 纪录片里，渔船在惊涛骇浪中艰难前行，让同学们看得屏住了呼吸。
example_en: In the documentary, a fishing vessel struggled to forge ahead through towering, ferocious waves — the pupils held their breath watching it.

meaning_zh: 雷电交加，即将下大雨的样子。
meaning_en: Lightning flashes and thunder roars.
example: 窗外电闪雷鸣，不一会儿就下起了大雨。
example_en: Outside the window, lightning flashed and thunder rumbled, and shortly afterwards the rain came pouring down.

meaning_zh: 炎热的太阳高高地挂在天上。
meaning_en: The sun is shining brightly.
example: 即使烈日当空，交警叔叔仍然坚守在岗位上。
example_en: Even under the blazing midday sun, the traffic warden remained steadfastly at his post without complaint.

meaning_zh: 思想认识一致，共同努力。
meaning_en: To make a concerted effort; to work in concert.
example: 只要大家齐心协力，就没有克服不了的困难。
example_en: As long as everyone pulls together, there is no difficulty that cannot be overcome.

meaning_zh: 交情很深，如同兄弟一样。
meaning_en: To be close like brothers.
example: 我和阿强从小在同一个组屋区长大，一起上学放学，情同手足。
example_en: Ah Qiang and I grew up in the same HDB estate, walked to school and back together every day — we are as close as brothers.

meaning_zh: 共同承担危险和困难。
meaning_en: To share weal and woe; to be together in times of trouble.
example: 小明和阿伟是患难与共的好朋友，小学六年里互相扶持，共同迎接PSLE。
example_en: Xiao Ming and Ah Wei are true friends who have been through thick and thin together, supporting each other all the way through six years of primary school as they prepared for the PSLE.

meaning_zh: 志趣相同，意见一致。
meaning_en: To have a common goal; to be kindred spirits.
example: 我们几个都热爱画漫画，志同道合，课余时间常常聚在图书馆一起创作。
example_en: The few of us all share a passion for drawing comics — kindred spirits who love to gather at the library during free time to create together.

meaning_zh: 彼此和好地相处。
meaning_en: To live in harmony; to get along peacefully.
example: 邻里之间应和睦相处，互相关心，遇到独居老人更要多加关照。
example_en: Neighbours should live in harmony and look out for one another — especially when it comes to elderly residents who live alone.

meaning_zh: 彼此帮助。
meaning_en: To help each other.
example: 同学之间要互相帮助，共同进步。
example_en: Classmates should help one another and work together towards shared progress.

meaning_zh: 又说又笑，气氛欢乐。
meaning_en: Talking and laughing; in a cheerful mood.
example: 小息时间，同学们在篮球场上追逐嬉戏，有说有笑，笑声传遍整个走廊。
example_en: During recess, the pupils chased and played on the basketball court, laughing and chatting — the sound of their laughter echoed all the way down the corridor.

meaning_zh: 为了一个目标，大家一起配合。
meaning_en: To unite and cooperate; to work together.
example: 这个任务需要大家团结合作才能完成。
example_en: This task can only be accomplished if everyone works together as a team.

meaning_zh: 共同经历患难。
meaning_en: To stand together through thick and thin.
example: 小强和阿伟从小学到中学一直风雨同舟，不论遇到什么困难都互相支持。
example_en: Xiao Qiang and Ah Wei have been through every storm together from primary to secondary school, always there for each other no matter what.

meaning_zh: 共同享受幸福，共同承担痛苦。
meaning_en: To share joys and hardships; to share weal and woe.
example: 备战校际辩论赛期间，教练和队员们同甘共苦，每天一起留校练习到天黑。
example_en: During preparations for the inter-school debate competition, the coach and the team shared every hardship, staying on after school together every day until nightfall.

meaning_zh: 真心待人。
meaning_en: To confide in someone; to treat someone with utmost sincerity.
example: 课余时，小丽和好友阿欣常常坐在组屋走廊，推心置腹地聊起彼此的烦恼。
example_en: After school, Xiao Li and her close friend Ah Xin would often sit on the corridor of the HDB block, sharing their innermost thoughts and worries with each other.

meaning_zh: 像形体和它的影子那样分不开。
meaning_en: Inseparable; always together.
example: 阿强和阿明这两个好朋友整天形影不离，连去食堂买饭都要等对方一起走。
example_en: Ah Qiang and Ah Ming are inseparable best friends — they even walk to the canteen together to buy their food.

meaning_zh: 以真心相见，互相坦诚。
meaning_en: To treat each other with absolute sincerity.
example: 真正的朋友应该肝胆相照，不论成败，都坦诚相待，绝不背后说坏话。
example_en: True friends should be completely open and honest with each other — loyal through and through, never speaking ill of one another behind their backs.

meaning_zh: 彼此心意一致，不用说出就能互相了解。
meaning_en: To share the same feelings; to have mutual affinity.
example: 小明和阿伟在篮球场上配合得天衣无缝，一个传球，一个上篮，真是心心相印。
example_en: Xiao Ming and Ah Wei had such a perfect understanding on the basketball court — one would pass and the other would already be in position for the lay-up.

meaning_zh: 关系非常密切，没有一点隔阂。
meaning_en: To be close friends; to be hand in glove.
example: 小慧和小玲是一对亲密无间的好朋友，课间分享零食，放学后一起搭地铁回家。
example_en: Xiao Hui and Xiao Ling are an inseparable pair — sharing snacks during recess and taking the MRT home together after school.

meaning_zh: 数量非常多。
meaning_en: Thousands upon thousands.
example: 国庆日庆典在滨海湾广场举行，成千上万的市民聚集在一起观看烟花表演。
example_en: Tens of thousands of people gathered at the Marina Bay waterfront on National Day to watch the fireworks display.

meaning_zh: 花样繁多，变化多端。
meaning_en: Multifarious; of a wide variety.
example: 小贩中心里的摊位五花八门，有炒粿条、印度煎饼、豆腐花，还有各种饮料，让人目不暇接。
example_en: The stalls in the hawker centre offered a dizzying variety of food — char kway teow, roti prata, tau huay, and all kinds of drinks — there was simply too much to take in at once.

meaning_zh: 具有多种多样的特征。
meaning_en: A great variety of; all sorts of.
example: 学校的课外活动各种各样，有华乐、合唱团、辩论队，还有田径队，让每个同学都能找到自己的兴趣。
example_en: The school's co-curricular activities are wonderfully varied — from Chinese Orchestra and Choir to Debate, and Track and Field — offering something for every pupil to discover their passion.

meaning_zh: 应该有的全都有，形容很齐全。
meaning_en: To have everything that one expects to find; to be complete.
example: 国家图书馆里各种类型的书籍应有尽有，是同学们课余自学的好地方。
example_en: The National Library has an extensive and comprehensive collection — whatever subject you are researching, you will find what you need there.

meaning_zh: 数都数不过来。
meaning_en: Countless; innumerable.
example: 傍晚时分，站在东海岸公园的海边，抬头望去，繁星点点，数不胜数。
example_en: Standing on the beach at East Coast Park as evening fell, we looked up to find the sky scattered with countless stars.

meaning_zh: 人聚集得非常多。
meaning_en: Huge crowds of people; a sea of people.
example: 国庆日那天，滨海湾一带人山人海，大家都聚集在那里等待烟花表演。
example_en: On National Day, the area around Marina Bay was packed with people as far as the eye could see, all gathered to watch the fireworks.

meaning_zh: 车马很多，形容热闹繁华。
meaning_en: Heavy traffic; a constant stream of traffic.
example: 每逢周末，乌节路一带总是车水马龙，购物的人群络绎不绝。
example_en: Every weekend, Orchard Road is a hive of activity — traffic flows steadily along the road and shoppers stream endlessly along the pavements.

meaning_zh: 满眼都是珍贵的东西。
meaning_en: A feast for the eyes; a superb collection of beautiful things.
example: 新加坡国际艺术节的展览馆里，各国艺术家的作品琳琅满目，让参观者目不暇接。
example_en: The exhibition hall at the Singapore International Arts Festival was filled with artworks from around the world — a dazzling array that left visitors spoilt for choice.

meaning_zh: 内容丰富，应有尽有。
meaning_en: All-embracing; all-inclusive.
example: 国家图书馆的馆藏包罗万象，不论是科学、历史还是文学，都能找到参考资料。
example_en: The National Library's collection is encyclopaedic in scope — whatever question you have, you will find an answer there.

meaning_zh: 内容丰富，形式多样。
meaning_en: Rich and varied; colorful.
example: 学校为同学们安排了丰富多彩的课外活动，有SYF表演、环保项目和社区服务。
example_en: The school has organised a rich and varied programme of extra-curricular activities, including SYF performances, environmental projects, and community service.

meaning_zh: 变化极多。
meaning_en: Ever-changing; to be constantly changing.
example: 天上的云彩千变万化，一会儿像马，一会儿像山。
example_en: The clouds above shifted and swirled in ever-changing forms — one moment they resembled a horse, the next a mountain.

meaning_zh: 行人车马来来往往，接连不断。
meaning_en: In an endless stream; continuously.
example: 国庆日那天，参观滨海湾浮动舞台展览的人络绎不绝，从早到晚人潮不断。
example_en: On National Day, visitors streamed into the exhibition at the Marina Bay Floating Platform without pause from morning till night.

meaning_zh: 连续不断。
meaning_en: In a steady and continuous stream.
example: 义工叔叔阿姨们源源不断地把捐来的物资送进社区关怀中心，温暖了许多有需要的家庭。
example_en: Volunteer uncles and aunties brought donated supplies to the community care centre in a steady, unceasing stream, bringing warmth to many families in need.

meaning_zh: 到处都是，形容非常多。
meaning_en: Can be found everywhere; common.
example: 像这样热心帮助邻居的好心人，在我们的组屋社区里比比皆是。
example_en: Kind-hearted people like him are everywhere in our HDB community — you only have to look.

meaning_zh: 数目多得无法计算。
meaning_en: Innumerable; countless.
example: 这场雨下了很久，地上的积水坑不计其数。
example_en: The rain had been falling for a long time, and puddles had formed all over the ground — far too many to count.

meaning_zh: 每天每月都有新的变化。
meaning_en: To change with each passing day; to make new progress.
example: 新加坡的发展日新月异，短短几年，滨海湾一带就建起了许多雄伟的地标，吸引了无数游客。
example_en: Singapore's development has been rapid and relentless — in just a few years, a cluster of impressive landmarks has risen around Marina Bay, drawing visitors from across the globe.

meaning_zh: 时间过得飞快。
meaning_en: Time flies like an arrow; how time flies.
example: 光阴似箭，转眼间六年小学生涯已经结束，我们即将踏上中学的新旅程。
example_en: Time flies — in the blink of an eye, six years of primary school have come to an end, and we are about to set off on the journey to secondary school.

meaning_zh: 不放过一分一秒，形容抓紧时间。
meaning_en: To race against time; to make the best use of one's time.
example: PSLE前的最后一个星期，同学们争分夺秒地复习，连小息时间也不放过。
example_en: In the final week before the PSLE, pupils were racing against the clock to revise, not even sparing a moment of recess.

meaning_zh: 形容极短的时间。
meaning_en: In the twinkling of an eye; very quickly.
example: 刚才还是晴空万里，转眼之间就乌云密布，下起了新加坡特有的午后雷阵雨。
example_en: The sky had been bright and clear just moments ago — in the blink of an eye it turned dark and overcast, and the familiar Singapore afternoon downpour began.

meaning_zh: 在极短的时间内变化快而多。
meaning_en: To change rapidly; to be fast-changing.
example: 新加坡的天气瞬息万变，刚才还阳光普照，转眼间就乌云密布，下起倾盆大雨来了。
example_en: Singapore's weather changes in the blink of an eye — one moment the sun is shining brightly, and the next the sky darkens and the rain comes bucketing down.

meaning_zh: 进步或发展极快。
meaning_en: At a tremendous pace; by leaps and bounds.
example: 新加坡的城市发展一日千里，滨海湾一带几年前还是空地，如今已耸立起许多地标建筑。
example_en: Singapore's urban development has advanced at a breathtaking pace — where there were open fields a few years ago around Marina Bay, landmark buildings now soar into the sky.

meaning_zh: 一天天、一月月地积累。
meaning_en: To accumulate bit by bit; by slow but steady accumulation.
example: 学好华文需要日积月累的功夫，每天坚持阅读报章是一个好方法。
example_en: Mastering Chinese takes time to build up day by day — reading a newspaper article every morning is one excellent way to do it.

meaning_zh: 一分一秒也要争取。
meaning_en: Every second counts; to seize every moment.
example: 救援人员接到求救电话后，立刻分秒必争地赶往现场，不敢有丝毫耽误。
example_en: The rescue team raced to the scene the moment the distress call came in, not daring to lose a single second.

meaning_zh: 随着时代的发展而不断发展、前进。
meaning_en: To keep abreast of the times; to move forward with the times.
example: 科技发展日新月异，我们的知识也要与时俱进，多关注身边新事物。
example_en: With technology evolving so rapidly, we must keep up with the times and stay curious about the world around us.

meaning_zh: 稍微一放松就消失了。
meaning_en: Transient; fleeting.
example: 参加SYF的机会稍纵即逝，每四年才一次，我们一定要全力以赴，不留遗憾。
example_en: The chance to perform at the Singapore Youth Festival comes only once every four years — we must give everything we have and leave no regrets.

meaning_zh: 用新的眼光来看待。
meaning_en: To sit up and take notice; to regard somebody with increased respect.
example: 小明这学期加入了华乐团后，华文和音乐成绩都大为进步，令老师和同学们刮目相看。
example_en: Since joining the Chinese Orchestra, Xiao Ming has made remarkable progress in both Chinese and music — everyone, including the teachers, now sees him in a completely new light.

meaning_zh: 主动要求承担任务。
meaning_en: To volunteer for; to offer to undertake.
example: 老师问谁愿意担任国庆日活动的主持人，小峰第一个自告奋勇地举起手来。
example_en: When the teacher asked for a volunteer to host the National Day celebration, Xiao Feng was the first to shoot his hand up.

meaning_zh: 非常留恋，舍不得分开。
meaning_en: To be reluctant to part; to hate to see someone go.
example: 小学毕业礼上，同学们依依不舍地互相拥抱，在校门前合影留念，不愿离去。
example_en: At the Primary Six graduation ceremony, classmates hugged one another with great reluctance, posing for photos at the school gate, unwilling to say goodbye.

meaning_zh: 得到的好处不少。
meaning_en: To gain a lot; to profit a great deal.
example: 参加了学校组织的社区探访活动后，我受益匪浅，更加懂得珍惜和感恩。
example_en: After taking part in the school's community outreach programme, I came away with a great deal to reflect on, and a much deeper appreciation for what I have.

meaning_zh: 艺术形象非常逼真，像活的一样。
meaning_en: Lifelike; vivid.
example: 艺术展上展出的那幅热带鱼水彩画栩栩如生，鱼鳞的光泽和水草的飘动都画得惟妙惟肖。
example_en: The watercolour painting of tropical fish at the art exhibition was breathtakingly lifelike — the shimmer of the scales and the sway of the water plants were captured with extraordinary skill.

meaning_zh: 认为自己正确，不接受别人意见。
meaning_en: To be opinionated; to be cocksure.
example: 我们不能自以为是，要多听听同学和老师的意见，才能不断进步。
example_en: We must never assume we are always right — listening humbly to the views of classmates and teachers is the only way to keep improving.

meaning_zh: 不认为是对的，表示不同意。
meaning_en: To object to; to take exception to.
example: 爸爸提醒他上网时间太长，他却不以为然，仍旧盯着平板电脑看视频。
example_en: Dad pointed out that he had been spending too long on screens, but he brushed it off without a care, and went straight back to watching videos on his tablet.

meaning_zh: 从道理上讲应当这样。
meaning_en: Of course; naturally; it goes without saying.
example: 作为学生，努力学习是理所当然的事情。
example_en: Studying hard is simply what is expected of a pupil — it is the most natural thing in the world.

meaning_zh: 没有事先约定而彼此意见一致。
meaning_en: To happen to coincide; to take the same action without prior consultation.
example: 老师公布可以提早放学的消息，全班同学不约而同地欢呼起来。
example_en: When the teacher announced they could leave early, the entire class burst into cheers simultaneously, without anyone planning it.

meaning_zh: 不知道怎么办才好。
meaning_en: To be at one's wit's end; to be at a loss.
example: 突然被老师点名上台朗读，他从来没有心理准备，一时不知所措，站在原地愣住了。
example_en: Being called to the front of the class without any warning to read aloud, he was caught completely off guard and stood rooted to the spot, not knowing what to do.

meaning_zh: 心里一点儿也不受感动。
meaning_en: To be aloof and indifferent; to be unconcerned.
example: 看到同学在食堂里向人借钱，我们不能无动于衷，应该主动关心，看看能否帮上忙。
example_en: When you see a classmate in difficulty at the canteen, you must not turn a blind eye — step forward and see if you can help.

meaning_zh: 无法想象，难以理解。
meaning_en: Inconceivable; unimaginable.
example: 他竟然在一分钟内跑完了400米，真是不可思议。
example_en: He completed the four-hundred-metre race in under a minute — it was truly beyond belief.

meaning_zh: 形容关怀、照顾得非常细心周到。
meaning_en: Meticulously; to take every care of someone.
example: 生病住院那几天，妈妈对我的照顾无微不至，从换药到喂饭，从没离开半步。
example_en: During the few days I was in hospital, Mum cared for me with meticulous, round-the-clock attention — from changing dressings to feeding me, she never once left my side.

meaning_zh: 心思不在这里。
meaning_en: Absent-minded; inattentive.
example: 老师正在讲解作文技巧，小明却心不在焉，脑子里想着下课后的篮球赛。
example_en: The teacher was explaining composition techniques, but Xiao Ming's mind was elsewhere — he was already thinking about the basketball match after school.

meaning_zh: 全部精神集中在一点上。
meaning_en: To be wholly absorbed; to devote one's mind to.
example: 图书馆里，小丽全神贯注地阅读着故事书，连旁边的同学轻声呼唤她也没察觉。
example_en: In the library, Xiao Li was so deeply absorbed in her storybook that she did not even notice a classmate softly calling her name from nearby.

meaning_zh: 牢记在心，时刻不忘。
meaning_en: To always remember; to dwell on.
example: 搬到新加坡多年的外公，至今仍念念不忘故乡的亲人，每逢节日都会拨电话问候。
example_en: Even after many years of living in Singapore, Granduncle still thinks constantly of the relatives back in his hometown, calling to wish them well every festive season.

meaning_zh: 没有办法，只能这样了。
meaning_en: To have no alternative; to be helpless.
example: 风筝线断了，断线的风筝飘向远处，弟弟无可奈何地望着它消失在组屋楼顶后面。
example_en: The kite string snapped and the kite drifted away on the wind — younger brother watched helplessly as it disappeared behind the rooftops of the HDB blocks.

meaning_zh: 理由充分，说话有气势。
meaning_en: To be in the right and self-confident; to speak with compelling argument.
example: 他清楚知道迟交功课是因为自己贪玩，但当老师问起时，仍理直气壮地解释说书包拉链坏了。
example_en: He knew full well that he had been playing around instead of doing his work, yet when the teacher asked, he boldly declared that his bag zip had broken — without a hint of remorse.

meaning_zh: 非常留恋，舍不得离开。
meaning_en: To be reluctant to part with; to hate to see someone go.
example: 在书展上看到那套精装故事书，他拿起来翻了又翻，临走时还恋恋不舍，不肯放下。
example_en: At the Book Fair, he picked up that deluxe storybook set and flicked through it again and again — even as he was leaving, he could not bring himself to put it down.

meaning_zh: 忍得再也无法忍受了。
meaning_en: To be driven beyond the limits of endurance; to have exhausted one's patience.
example: 同桌一而再、再而三地拿他的文具不归还，他终于忍无可忍，直接向老师反映了。
example_en: After his deskmate repeatedly borrowed his stationery and never returned it, he finally reached the end of his patience and brought the matter to the teacher.

meaning_zh: 好心好意地再三劝告。
meaning_en: To counsel somebody patiently and with good intention.
example: 妈妈苦口婆心地劝小峰减少打游戏的时间，专心备考PSLE，他就是听不进去。
example_en: Mum pleaded with Xiao Feng again and again to cut down on gaming and focus on preparing for the PSLE — but he simply would not listen.

meaning_zh: 感激的心情无法完全表达出来。
meaning_en: To be extremely grateful; to be overwhelmed with gratitude.
example: 林老师在我最艰难的时候坚持辅导我，帮助我通过了华文考试，我对她感激不尽。
example_en: Teacher Lim stood by me and tutored me through my most difficult period, helping me pass my Chinese exam — I cannot thank her enough.

meaning_zh: 想了又想，反复思考。
meaning_en: To turn over in one's mind; to ponder.
example: 他左思右想，终于想出了一个好办法。
example_en: He turned the problem over and over in his mind, and eventually hit upon a brilliant solution.

meaning_zh: 想尽一切办法。
meaning_en: To use every possible means; to do everything possible.
example: 班主任千方百计地为学习有困难的同学安排补课和配对辅导，希望每个人都能进步。
example_en: The form teacher went to every length to arrange extra lessons and paired tutoring for pupils who were struggling, hoping that no one would be left behind.

meaning_zh: 眼睛看见复杂纷繁的东西而感到迷乱。
meaning_en: To be dazzled; to be confused by a variety of things.
example: 书展上各类书籍、文具和精美礼品摆满了整个展览厅，看得人眼花缭乱。
example_en: At the Book Fair, the shelves were packed with books, stationery, and attractive gifts — there was so much to take in that it made your head spin.

meaning_zh: 抑制不住自己的感情。
meaning_en: To be seized with a sudden emotion; cannot help doing something.
example: 看到老师在毕业礼上哽咽地说再见，许多同学情不自禁地流下了眼泪。
example_en: When the teacher choked up saying her goodbyes at the graduation ceremony, many pupils could not help but let their tears fall.

meaning_zh: 不住口地称赞。
meaning_en: To be profuse in praise; to praise endlessly.
example: 妈妈把煮好的咖喱鸡端到组屋楼下和邻居们分享，大家吃了都赞不绝口。
example_en: Mum brought a pot of home-made curry chicken to share with the neighbours downstairs, and everyone who tasted it could not stop singing its praises.

meaning_zh: 事情已到眼前，非常紧迫。
meaning_en: Extremely urgent; imminent.
example: 期末考试迫在眉睫，同学们纷纷留在图书馆复习，不敢有丝毫懈怠。
example_en: With the final exams just around the corner, pupils stayed on in the library to revise, not daring to let up for a moment.

meaning_zh: 出乎人们的意料。
meaning_en: To be beyond one's expectations; to come as a surprise.
example: 没有人看好这所邻里小学的合唱团，但他们在SYF上勇夺金奖，真是出人意料。
example_en: Nobody had expected much from the neighbourhood school's choir, but they went on to win the gold award at the Singapore Youth Festival — a truly astonishing result.

meaning_zh: 气氛热烈，情绪高涨。
meaning_en: In full swing; buzzing with activity.
example: 学校开放日的布置工作进行得热火朝天，同学们分工合作，个个干劲十足。
example_en: Preparations for the school's Open House were in full swing — pupils worked in teams with great enthusiasm, and every one of them threw themselves into the task.

meaning_zh: 出汗很多，湿透了背上的衣服。
meaning_en: To be drenched in sweat; to sweat profusely.
example: 体育课跑步训练结束，小峰热得汗流浃背，立刻跑去喝水，才感到舒服一些。
example_en: After the running drills in Physical Education, Xiao Feng was drenched in sweat all down his back and made straight for the water station to cool down.

meaning_zh: 有条理，丝毫不乱。
meaning_en: In perfect order; orderly; neat and tidy.
example: 小红的学习用品和书本总是整理得井井有条，书包一打开就知道该拿哪一本。
example_en: Xiao Hong always keeps her school bag impeccably organised — the moment you open it, you know exactly where every book is.

meaning_zh: 话很多，说起来没完没了。
meaning_en: To talk on and on in a flow of eloquence.
example: 一谈到他热爱的乐高积木，阿明就滔滔不绝地说个不停，连小息时间都讲不完。
example_en: The moment the topic turned to his beloved LEGO sets, Ah Ming could talk non-stop for ages — he was still going strong even when recess was over.

meaning_zh: 瞪大眼睛说不出话来。
meaning_en: Gaping; dumbstruck; stupefied.
example: 魔术师在学校庆典上变出一只真兔子，全场同学目瞪口呆，掌声雷动。
example_en: When the magician produced a real rabbit at the school celebration, the entire audience stared open-mouthed in astonishment, and the hall erupted in applause.

meaning_zh: 像哑巴一样说不出话来。
meaning_en: To be rendered speechless; to be left without an argument.
example: 老师把证据一一摆出来，他才哑口无言，只好低下头承认错误。
example_en: When the teacher laid out the evidence one piece at a time, he was left completely speechless and had no choice but to bow his head and admit his mistake.

meaning_zh: 非常担心或害怕。
meaning_en: To be on tenterhooks; to be scared stiff.
example: 他不小心把班级的仙人掌盆栽打碎了，一直提心吊胆地等着老师回来，不知如何开口。
example_en: He had accidentally knocked over the class cactus before the teacher returned, and spent the rest of the afternoon on tenterhooks, dreading the moment he would have to explain.

meaning_zh: 一眼就能看清楚。
meaning_en: To be obvious; to be clear at a glance.
example: 老师把这学期各科的成绩制成图表，每个人的进退步情况一目了然。
example_en: The teacher displayed each pupil's results in a chart, making it immediately clear at a glance who had improved and who had dropped.

meaning_zh: 形容战斗或劳动前精神振奋的样子。
meaning_en: To be itching for a fight; to be eager to start.
example: 参加校际田径赛前，运动员们在热身区摩拳擦掌，个个跃跃欲试，准备大显身手。
example_en: In the warm-up area before the inter-school athletics meet, the athletes were limbering up and raring to go, each one eager to shine on the track.

meaning_zh: 环境非常清洁。
meaning_en: Spotless; as clean as a whistle.
example: 值日生把教室打扫得一尘不染，连讲台的角落都擦得干干净净。
example_en: The pupils on duty swept and wiped every corner of the classroom until it was spotlessly clean, not leaving so much as a speck of dust on the whiteboard ledge.

meaning_zh: 心神不安。
meaning_en: To be agitated; to be in a mental turmoil.
example: 马上就要轮到我上台演讲了，我心里七上八下，双手不停地搓着演讲稿。
example_en: It was almost my turn to go on stage to deliver my speech, and my heart was in my mouth — I kept shuffling my script nervously with trembling hands.

meaning_zh: 高兴到极点，手乱舞，脚乱跳。
meaning_en: To dance for joy; to gesture excitedly.
example: 听到老师宣布下周要去东海岸公园进行户外学习之旅，同学们高兴得手舞足蹈。
example_en: When the teacher announced that next week's lesson would be an outdoor learning journey to East Coast Park, the whole class cheered and jumped for joy.

meaning_zh: 形容非常吃惊。
meaning_en: To be greatly surprised; to be taken aback.
example: 正在看书的我大吃一惊，原来是邻居小弟弟悄悄地躲在门后，突然跳出来吓我。
example_en: I was sitting quietly reading when I nearly jumped out of my skin — it turned out the little boy from next door had hidden behind the door and leapt out to give me a fright.

meaning_zh: 心里急得像火烧一样。
meaning_en: To be burning with impatience; to be extremely anxious.
example: 巴士眼看就要开了，爸爸还没到站，我心急如焚，不停地往路口张望。
example_en: The bus was about to pull away and Dad still had not reached the stop — I was frantic with worry, craning my neck towards the road junction every few seconds.

meaning_zh: 形容受到惊吓或震惊，说不出话来。
meaning_en: To be dumbfounded; to be left speechless with shock.
example: 魔术师变出一只真兔子，台下的同学们个个目瞪口呆，半天才回过神来。
example_en: The magician produced a real rabbit, and the pupils in the audience were left completely dumbfounded, taking a long while to come back to their senses.

meaning_zh: 形容理屈词穷，无话可说。
meaning_en: To be at a loss for words; to be rendered speechless.
example: 老师把证据一一摆出，他被问得哑口无言，只好低着头承认错误。
example_en: The teacher presented the evidence one by one, and he was left completely speechless, bowing his head and admitting his mistake.

meaning_zh: 形容情绪低落，失去信心的样子。
meaning_en: Crestfallen; dejected; in low spirits.
example: 小华没有入选学校合唱团，垂头丧气地走出音乐室，妈妈连忙上前安慰他。
example_en: Xiao Hua was not selected for the school choir and walked out of the music room crestfallen — his mum quickly stepped forward to comfort him.

meaning_zh: 遇到出乎意料的好事，心里非常高兴。
meaning_en: Overjoyed by an unexpected pleasant surprise.
example: 老师宣布班上获得了校园清洁比赛第一名，同学们喜出望外，欢呼雀跃。
example_en: When the teacher announced that their class had won first place in the school cleanliness competition, the pupils were overjoyed and cheered with delight.

meaning_zh: 形容心神不安，坐立不定，十分担忧。
meaning_en: Anxious and uneasy; on tenterhooks.
example: 成绩单快要发下来了，小玲忐忑不安地坐在座位上，手心都出汗了。
example_en: The report cards were about to be handed out, and Xiao Ling sat at her desk on tenterhooks, her palms sweating with anxiety.

meaning_zh: 形容愁苦、忧愁的神情。
meaning_en: To look worried and unhappy; to wear a long face.
example: 阿强因为作文不及格，整天愁眉苦脸，连饭都吃不下。
example_en: Ah Qiang failed his composition and walked around with a long face all day, unable to even eat his meals.

meaning_zh: 形容非常愤怒的样子。
meaning_en: Furious; seething with anger.
example: 小峰发现有人偷吃了他的点心，怒气冲冲地在课室里找了一圈。
example_en: Xiao Feng discovered that someone had eaten his snack and stormed around the classroom in a fury looking for the culprit.

meaning_zh: 形容哭泣时眼泪流了很多，沾满脸颊。
meaning_en: With tears streaming down one's face; to weep profusely.
example: 毕业典礼上，老师深情地说着道别的话，许多同学感动得泪流满面。
example_en: At the graduation ceremony, the teacher said farewell with great warmth and feeling, and many pupils were so moved that tears streamed down their faces.

meaning_zh: 形容高兴到了极点，像发狂一样。
meaning_en: Wild with joy; ecstatically happy.
example: 小慧得知自己考进了直通车课程，欣喜若狂，立刻打电话告诉爷爷奶奶。
example_en: Xiao Hui learnt that she had been accepted into the Integrated Programme and was absolutely wild with joy, immediately calling her grandparents to share the news.

meaning_zh: 形容非常惊慌，不知道该怎么办。
meaning_en: To panic and lose one's head; to be thrown into a fluster.
example: 火警铃突然响起，还没有经历过演练的新生们惊慌失措，幸好老师及时安抚大家。
example_en: The fire alarm suddenly went off and the new pupils who had not been through a drill before panicked and lost their heads — fortunately the teacher quickly calmed everyone down.

meaning_zh: 形容非常害怕，提心吊胆。
meaning_en: To be trembling with fear; terrified.
example: 小丽第一次在全校面前上台演讲，胆战心惊，双腿像灌了铅一样沉重。
example_en: Xiao Li was giving a speech in front of the whole school for the first time and was trembling with fear, her legs feeling as heavy as lead.

meaning_zh: 形容心神不安，坐立不安，非常难受。
meaning_en: To be on pins and needles; extremely restless and uncomfortable.
example: 考卷发下来前，小明如坐针毡，心里一直盘算着自己那道大题做对了没有。
example_en: Before the exam papers were returned, Xiao Ming was on pins and needles, his mind going over and over whether he had got that big question right.

meaning_zh: 形容既后悔又恨自己，感到十分痛苦。
meaning_en: Filled with remorse and regret.
example: 小伟因为贪玩没有复习，考试失败后悔恨交加，暗自发誓下次一定要认真备考。
example_en: Xiao Wei had neglected his revision to play and failed the exam — he was filled with remorse and silently vowed to prepare properly next time.

meaning_zh: 形容感到非常羞耻和惭愧，难以面对。
meaning_en: Overcome with shame; unable to face others.
example: 小华说谎被老师当众点名批评，羞愧难当，脸涨得通红，恨不得找个地缝钻进去。
example_en: Xiao Hua was called out by name in front of the class for lying and was overcome with shame — his face flushed deep red and he wished he could sink into the floor.

meaning_zh: 形容非常感激，感动得流下眼泪。
meaning_en: So grateful as to be moved to tears.
example: 林老师在小明最困难的时候给予支持，小明感激涕零，写了一封亲笔信向老师道谢。
example_en: Teacher Lim gave Xiao Ming support at his most difficult moment, and he was so moved with gratitude that he wrote a handwritten letter to thank her.

meaning_zh: 形容极度感谢，感激之情非常深厚。
meaning_en: Extremely grateful; deeply thankful.
example: 邻居阿姨在妈妈生病时帮忙照顾弟弟妹妹，全家人对她万分感激。
example_en: The neighbour auntie helped look after the younger siblings when Mum was ill, and the whole family was extremely grateful to her.

meaning_zh: 形容受到意外的优待或重视而感到惊喜。
meaning_en: Overwhelmed by an unexpected honour or favour.
example: 小明被选为学校代表出席颁奖典礼，受宠若惊，连忙向老师道谢。
example_en: Xiao Ming was chosen to represent the school at the prize-giving ceremony and was overwhelmed by the unexpected honour, immediately thanking the teacher.

meaning_zh: 形容非常伤心，哭得很厉害。
meaning_en: To cry bitterly; to weep and sob uncontrollably.
example: 小玲得知爷爷病重住院，当场痛哭流涕，同学们都上前安慰她。
example_en: When Xiao Ling learnt that her grandfather had been taken seriously ill and hospitalised, she burst into bitter sobs on the spot — her classmates all came forward to comfort her.

meaning_zh: 形容对不公正的事情感到非常愤慨。
meaning_en: To be filled with righteous indignation.
example: 看到校园霸凌事件，同学们义愤填膺，纷纷站出来为受害者说话。
example_en: Witnessing the bullying incident, the pupils were filled with righteous indignation and stood up one after another to speak out for the victim.

meaning_zh: 形容极度愤怒。
meaning_en: Furious to the point of one's hair standing on end.
example: 阿强得知有人散布关于他的谣言，怒发冲冠，立刻要求对方公开道歉。
example_en: Ah Qiang learnt that someone had been spreading rumours about him and was absolutely furious, immediately demanding a public apology.

meaning_zh: 做事做到一半就放弃，不能坚持到底。
meaning_en: To give up halfway; to leave something unfinished.
example: 练习书法最怕半途而废，小丽坚持每天练习，终于写出了一手漂亮的字。
example_en: The biggest pitfall in practising calligraphy is giving up halfway — Xiao Li persisted with her daily practice and eventually developed beautiful handwriting.

meaning_zh: 比喻做事情快要成功时，因最后一步没做好而失败。
meaning_en: To fail at the last step; to fall at the final hurdle.
example: 我们队在辩论赛决赛中只差最后一轮就能夺冠，却因准备不足而功亏一篑，十分可惜。
