example_en: Our team was just one round away from winning the debate championship but fell at the final hurdle due to insufficient preparation — it was a great shame.

meaning_zh: 之前所做的努力全部白费了。
meaning_en: To have all one's previous efforts come to nothing.
example: 小伟辛苦备战了三个月的田径赛，结果临场受伤，前功尽弃，令人惋惜。
example_en: Xiao Wei had trained hard for three months for the athletics competition, but was injured on the day — all his previous efforts had come to nothing, and it was truly a pity.

meaning_zh: 所得到的好处不能弥补所受到的损失。
meaning_en: The gain does not make up for the loss; not worth the cost.
example: 为了早点完成作业而草草了事，结果错误百出被老师要求重做，真是得不偿失。
example_en: Rushing through his homework just to finish early only led to so many mistakes that the teacher made him redo it — the gain simply did not make up for the loss.

meaning_zh: 因为某个错误或疏忽，影响了整个人生。
meaning_en: To affect one's whole life due to a mistake or negligence.
example: 老师常提醒我们，不认真对待学习可能贻误终身，所以要从小养成好习惯。
example_en: The teacher often reminds us that not taking our studies seriously can have lifelong consequences — so we must develop good habits from a young age.

meaning_zh: 抓住时机，立刻作出决断。
meaning_en: To make a prompt decision; to act decisively when the opportunity arises.
example: 看到同学突然晕倒，班长当机立断，立刻通知老师并拨打急救电话。
example_en: When a classmate suddenly fainted, the class monitor acted without hesitation — immediately notifying the teacher and calling for an ambulance.

meaning_zh: 形容勇敢地站出来，承担责任或帮助别人。
meaning_en: To step forward bravely; to stand up and be counted.
example: 当有同学被欺负时，阿明挺身而出，勇敢地为同学说话。
example_en: When a classmate was being bullied, Ah Ming stepped forward bravely and stood up for his friend.

meaning_zh: 形容不顾自身安危，勇往直前。
meaning_en: To throw caution to the wind; to act bravely without regard for one's own safety.
example: 消防员叔叔奋不顾身地冲入火场，将被困的老人背出来，令众人肃然起敬。
example_en: The firefighter charged into the blazing building without regard for his own safety to carry the trapped elderly man out, earning the deep respect of all present.

meaning_zh: 形容态度镇定，不慌不忙。
meaning_en: Calm and unhurried; composed under pressure.
example: 上台演讲时，小慧从容不迫地讲完整篇演讲稿，赢得全场掌声。
example_en: When she took the stage for her speech, Xiao Hui delivered the entire address with calm composure, earning applause from the whole audience.

meaning_zh: 形容事情很容易做到，轻而易举。
meaning_en: Easy to accomplish; a piece of cake.
example: 阿强平时勤练数学，期末考试对他来说简直是手到擒来。
example_en: Ah Qiang practised Mathematics diligently throughout the year, so the final exam was an absolute piece of cake for him.

meaning_zh: 面对危险或紧急情况，仍能保持镇定，不慌乱。
meaning_en: To remain calm in the face of danger.
example: 学校消防演习中，值日班长临危不乱，有条不紊地带领全班疏散到集合点。
example_en: During the school fire drill, the duty class monitor remained calm under pressure and led the whole class in an orderly evacuation to the assembly point.

meaning_zh: 形容不停地赶路或做事，没有休息。
meaning_en: Without stopping; at full speed without a break.
example: 为了在截止日期前完成壁报，同学们马不停蹄地工作，终于在放学前完成了。
example_en: To finish the display board before the deadline, the pupils worked non-stop and finally completed it before school ended.

literal_zh: 像雷那样猛烈像风那样快
meaning_zh: 形容执行命令或做事迅速有力，干脆利落。
meaning_en: Swift and decisive; to act with speed and force.
example: 新班长雷厉风行，上任第一天就整顿好了课室的纪律问题。
example_en: The new class monitor was swift and decisive — on the very first day in the role, discipline in the classroom was sorted out.

meaning_zh: 形容能够独立承担某一方面的任务。
meaning_en: To be able to take charge independently; to hold one's own.
example: 阿伟在学生领袖训练营中表现出色，已经能够独当一面，负责统筹各项活动。
example_en: Ah Wei performed outstandingly at the student leadership camp and was already able to take charge independently, coordinating the various activities on his own.

meaning_zh: 形容平时默默无闻，突然有出色的表现令人刮目相看。
meaning_en: To amaze everyone with a single brilliant performance; to burst onto the scene.
example: 小丽平时沉默寡言，却在朗诵比赛中一鸣惊人，夺得全校冠军。
example_en: Xiao Li was quiet and unassuming in everyday life, but amazed everyone at the recitation competition by taking first place in the whole school.

meaning_zh: 遇到应该做的事，就勇敢地承担，不推辞。
meaning_en: To take on a responsibility without shirking; to step up when duty calls.
example: 老师问谁愿意帮助新转学的同学，小明当仁不让，主动举手承担了这个任务。
example_en: When the teacher asked who would be willing to help the new transfer pupil settle in, Xiao Ming stepped up without hesitation and volunteered for the task.

literal_zh: 见到正义的事勇敢去做
meaning_zh: 看到正义的事情，勇敢地去做。
meaning_en: To act bravely for a just cause; to stand up for what is right.
example: 阿强看到陌生叔叔在地铁站晕倒，见义勇为，上前协助并通知地铁站工作人员。
example_en: Ah Qiang saw a stranger collapse at the MRT station and bravely stepped forward to help, assisting him and alerting the station staff.

meaning_zh: 比喻只要坚持不懈，细微的力量也能做到看似不可能的事。
meaning_en: Persistent effort can achieve the impossible; constant dripping wears away the stone.
example: 阿明英语底子差，但他相信滴水穿石，每天坚持背单词，半年后成绩大有进步。
example_en: Ah Ming had a weak foundation in English, but he believed that persistent effort wears away the stone — he memorised vocabulary every day and his results improved greatly within half a year.

meaning_zh: 不畏困难，勇敢地向前迈进。
meaning_en: To press forward bravely; to forge ahead with courage.
example: 面对PSLE的压力，小慧勇往直前，从未想过放弃，终于取得了优异的成绩。
example_en: Facing the pressure of the PSLE, Xiao Hui pressed forward bravely and never once thought of giving up, eventually achieving excellent results.

meaning_zh: 形容做事认真、踏实，一步一步地去做。
meaning_en: Down-to-earth; steady and practical in one's approach.
example: 小明不求走捷径，脚踏实地地完成每一道练习题，成绩稳步提升。
example_en: Xiao Ming never looked for shortcuts — he worked through every practice question in a steady, down-to-earth manner, and his results improved steadily.

meaning_zh: 用全部的力量去做某件事。
meaning_en: To give one's all; to go all out.
example: 校际辩论赛就在眼前，队员们全力以赴，每天放学后留下来练习到天黑。
example_en: The inter-school debate competition was just around the corner, and the team members gave their all — staying behind to practise every day until nightfall.

meaning_zh: 坚持不断地努力，从不懈怠。
meaning_en: To strive without letting up; to make unceasing effort.
example: 小玲不懈努力，每天提早半小时到学校温习，华文成绩从及格提高到了优秀。
example_en: Xiao Ling strived without letting up, arriving half an hour early at school every day to revise — her Chinese results rose from a pass to an excellent grade.

meaning_zh: 努力向上，不断改善自己，永不停止。
meaning_en: To strive constantly for self-improvement; never to cease making an effort.
example: 阿伟家境虽然不富裕，但他自强不息，靠奖学金完成了学业，令全家引以为荣。
example_en: Ah Wei's family was not well-off, but he never stopped striving — he funded his studies through a scholarship and made the whole family proud.

meaning_zh: 已经很好了，还要求做得更好。
meaning_en: To constantly strive for improvement; never satisfied with good enough.
example: 小慧的作文已经写得很好，但她精益求精，反复修改，力求每一句都更加生动。
example_en: Xiao Hui's composition was already very well written, but she was never satisfied with good enough — she revised it repeatedly, striving to make every sentence more vivid.

meaning_zh: 面对困难不退缩，勇敢地迎接挑战。
meaning_en: To face difficulties head-on; to tackle challenges with courage.
example: 参加科学项目比赛时，小组遇到了重重困难，但大家迎难而上，最终完成了实验。
example_en: The group encountered many difficulties during the science project competition, but they faced every challenge head-on and eventually completed the experiment.

meaning_zh: 比喻不畏艰险，克服重重困难，勇往直前。
meaning_en: To hack through thorns and brambles; to forge ahead through every obstacle.
example: 华乐团备战SYF的过程艰辛无比，但团员们披荆斩棘，最终摘得金奖。
example_en: The journey preparing for SYF was gruelling, but the Chinese Orchestra members hacked through every obstacle and ultimately brought home the gold award.

meaning_zh: 互相守护帮助，共同应对困难。
meaning_en: To watch over and help one another; mutual support in the community.
example: 我们组屋区的邻居守望相助，疫情期间互相帮忙采购日用品，令人感动。
example_en: The neighbours in our HDB estate watched over and helped one another — during the pandemic they took turns buying daily necessities for each other, which was truly touching.

meaning_zh: 从自身的感受推想到别人，设身处地为他人着想。
meaning_en: To put oneself in another's shoes; to extend one's feelings to others.
example: 小丽懂得推己及人，她知道自己不喜欢被孤立，所以总是主动邀请独坐的同学一起玩。
example_en: Xiao Li knew how to put herself in others' shoes — knowing she would not like to be left out, she always took the initiative to invite classmates sitting alone to join in.

meaning_zh: 比喻在困难中互相帮助，齐心协力，共度难关。
meaning_en: To be in the same boat; to pull together through difficulties.
example: 班上有几位同学生病了，其余的同学同舟共济，帮他们补上落下的课程笔记。
example_en: When several classmates fell ill, the rest of the class pulled together and helped them catch up on the notes they had missed.

meaning_zh: 形容在生死关头都能共同面对的深厚友谊。
meaning_en: Friends who would face life and death together; a bond forged through hardship.
example: 阿明和阿强从小学到中学互相扶持，早已结成生死之交，彼此的家人也像亲人一样。
example_en: Ah Ming and Ah Qiang supported each other from primary through secondary school — they had long since forged a bond that could weather anything, and each other's families were like their own.

meaning_zh: 形容为朋友甘愿付出极大的牺牲。
meaning_en: To go through fire and water for a friend; to make any sacrifice for friendship.
example: 阿伟对朋友真诚，每次有困难他都两肋插刀，从不计较个人得失。
example_en: Ah Wei was utterly sincere with his friends — whenever anyone was in trouble, he would go through fire and water for them without a thought for his own interests.

meaning_zh: 互相补充，互相促进，共同完成某件事。
meaning_en: To complement each other; to be mutually reinforcing.
example: 小明的华文好，阿强的英文强，两人相辅相成，学习上互相帮助，进步很快。
example_en: Xiao Ming was strong in Chinese and Ah Qiang excelled in English — the two complemented each other perfectly, helping one another in their studies and making rapid progress.

meaning_zh: 比喻互相赠答，以礼相待，投之以好意，报之以好意。
meaning_en: To return kindness for kindness; to reciprocate goodwill.
example: 小玲帮阿欣补习数学，阿欣投桃报李，帮小玲练习英文口语，两人互相成长。
example_en: Xiao Ling helped Ah Xin with Maths, and Ah Xin returned the favour by helping Xiao Ling practise English speaking — the two of them grew together.

meaning_zh: 帮助处于危难或困境中的人。
meaning_en: To help those in danger and relieve those in need.
example: 社区关怀角的义工们长期扶危济困，为独居老人和有需要的家庭送去温暖。
example_en: The volunteers at the community care corner have long been helping those in need, bringing warmth to elderly residents living alone and families facing hardship.

literal_zh: 同样优秀的人互相爱惜
meaning_zh: 形容有才能的人互相欣赏，惺惺相惜。
meaning_en: A mutual appreciation between kindred spirits.
example: 小慧和小玲都热爱写作，两人惺惺相惜，经常互相分享作文心得。
example_en: Xiao Hui and Xiao Ling both had a passion for writing — kindred spirits who cherished each other's talent and often shared their thoughts on composition.

meaning_zh: 大家同心合力，就能克服任何困难。
meaning_en: Unity is strength; when everyone is united, any obstacle can be overcome.
example: 班上同学众志成城，齐心合力清洁组屋走廊，为社区服务日活动赢得了好评。
example_en: The class was united as one — they worked together to clean the HDB corridor and received great praise for their Community Day effort.

meaning_zh: 形容言行一致，外表和内心都一样，不虚伪。
meaning_en: Consistent inside and out; what you see is what you get.
example: 小明是个表里如一的人，他说要帮忙，就真的动手去做，从来不说空话。
example_en: Xiao Ming is a person who is consistent inside and out — whenever he says he will help, he actually rolls up his sleeves and does it, never just paying lip service.

meaning_zh: 说出口的话，一定要去做到，讲信用。
meaning_en: A person of one's word; to do as one says.
example: 阿强答应帮同学补习就绝不爽约，他言出必行，同学们都很信任他。
example_en: Ah Qiang never broke a promise when he agreed to tutor a classmate — he was a man of his word, and his classmates trusted him completely.

meaning_zh: 设身处地为别人着想，体谅别人的感受。
meaning_en: To put oneself in another's place; to feel for others.
example: 小丽懂得将心比心，看到同学哭泣，她不会嘲笑，而是轻声问对方需要什么帮助。
example_en: Xiao Li knows how to put herself in others' shoes — when she sees a classmate crying, she never mocks them but quietly asks what kind of help they need.

meaning_zh: 严格约束自己，全心全意为公众服务。
meaning_en: To subordinate personal interests to the public good; selflessly devoted.
example: 社区居委会的王叔叔克己奉公，放弃了周末休息时间，义务为居民调解纠纷。
example_en: Uncle Wang from the residents' committee was selflessly devoted to the community — he gave up his weekends to mediate disputes for residents without any reward.

meaning_zh: 品德高尚的人，能包容和承载一切。
meaning_en: A person of virtue can bear all things; moral strength sustains everything.
example: 校长以厚德载物教导我们，一个有品德的人，才能在社会上受人尊重。
example_en: The principal taught us that only a person of genuine virtue earns true respect in society.

literal_zh: 见到贤人就想向他看齐
meaning_zh: 看到品德高尚或有才能的人，就想向他学习，赶上他。
meaning_en: To look up to and emulate the virtuous; to aspire to be as good as those one admires.
example: 小慧见贤思齐，从林老师身上学到了认真负责的态度，立志将来也要成为一名好老师。
example_en: Xiao Hui looked to her role models for inspiration — she learnt a conscientious and responsible attitude from Teacher Lim and aspired to become a good teacher herself one day.

meaning_zh: 形容别人给予的恩情和帮助非常深厚。
meaning_en: A great debt of gratitude; a profound kindness bestowed.
example: 陈老师对小明有大恩大德，当年正是她的鼓励让他重拾了对华文的信心。
example_en: Teacher Chen's kindness to Xiao Ming was immense — it was her encouragement that restored his confidence in Chinese all those years ago.

literal_zh: 防止微小问题阻断渐进
meaning_zh: 在坏事刚刚出现苗头时，就加以防止和纠正，不让它发展。
meaning_en: To nip problems in the bud; to prevent small issues from growing.
example: 老师教导我们要防微杜渐，不要因为小小的不良习惯而影响未来的发展。
example_en: The teacher taught us to nip problems in the bud — never let a small bad habit grow into something that could affect our future.

meaning_zh: 用礼貌和尊重的态度对待他人。
meaning_en: To treat others with courtesy and respect.
example: 小杰虽然是转校生，但他对每一位同学都以礼相待，很快就融入了班集体。
example_en: Even as a new transfer pupil, Xiao Jie treated every classmate with courtesy and respect, and quickly became part of the class.

meaning_zh: 努力学习，刻苦练习。
meaning_en: To study diligently and practise hard.
example: 阿强勤学苦练，每天放学后在家练习钢琴一小时，终于在音乐节上获得了优秀奖。
example_en: Ah Qiang studied diligently and practised hard — he played the piano for an hour every day after school and eventually won an excellent award at the music festival.

meaning_zh: 注意力高度集中，全神贯注地做某件事。
meaning_en: To concentrate wholeheartedly; to be completely focused.
example: 考试时，小明专心致志地作答，把周围的嘈杂声全都置之不理。
example_en: During the exam, Xiao Ming concentrated wholeheartedly on his paper, completely blocking out all the noise around him.

meaning_zh: 非常喜欢学习，不感到疲倦厌烦。
meaning_en: To love learning tirelessly; insatiably curious.
example: 林老师好学不倦，退休后仍坚持每天阅读，常与我们分享最新的知识。
example_en: Teacher Lim's love of learning was tireless — even in retirement she read every day and often shared the latest knowledge she had picked up with us.

meaning_zh: 读书总是有好处的，只要读书就能获益。
meaning_en: Reading is always beneficial; there is profit in opening a book.
example: 爷爷常说开卷有益，从小就鼓励我多读课外书，增广见闻。
example_en: Grandfather always said that reading is never wasted — he encouraged me from a young age to read widely and broaden my horizons.

literal_zh: 厚厚积累然后薄薄发出
meaning_zh: 长期积累知识和力量，在适当时机发挥出来。
meaning_en: To accumulate knowledge over time and release it at the right moment; to bide one's time.
example: 小丽写作文从不急于求成，厚积薄发，三年的阅读积累让她在升中考试中发挥出色。
example_en: Xiao Li never rushed her writing — she had been quietly building her reserves of reading for three years, and it all paid off in a stellar performance in her secondary school entrance exam.

meaning_zh: 努力赶上，不甘落后。
meaning_en: To catch up fast; to close the gap with determination.
example: 小峰刚转校时成绩落后，但他发愤用功，很快就迎头赶上，成为班上名列前茅的学生。
example_en: When Xiao Feng first transferred schools his results were behind, but he threw himself into his studies and quickly caught up — soon becoming one of the top pupils in the class.

meaning_zh: 形容进步非常迅速，发展很快。
meaning_en: To advance by leaps and bounds; rapid and dramatic progress.
example: 小明加入华乐团后，华文阅读能力突飞猛进，作文也越写越生动。
example_en: After joining the Chinese Orchestra, Xiao Ming's Chinese reading ability advanced by leaps and bounds, and his compositions became increasingly vivid.

literal_zh: 只知道一半只理解一半
meaning_zh: 形容知道得不全面，理解得不够透彻。
meaning_en: To have only a superficial understanding; to know something only halfway.
example: 小华对这道科学题一知半解，回答时漏掉了关键步骤，结果失了分。
example_en: Xiao Hua had only a superficial understanding of the science question and left out a key step in his answer, losing marks as a result.

literal_zh: 白马从缝隙间飞驰而过
meaning_zh: 比喻时间过得很快，像白马在缝隙间飞驰而过。
meaning_en: Time flies like a white horse flashing past a crack in the wall.
example: 白驹过隙，转眼间我们在小学度过了六年美好的时光，如今即将踏入中学。
example_en: Time has flown by like a white horse past a crack — in the blink of an eye we have spent six wonderful years in primary school and are now about to step into secondary school.

literal_zh: 岁月像织布梭子一样快
meaning_zh: 形容时间过得很快，像织布的梭子一样飞速流逝。
meaning_en: Time flies like a weaver's shuttle.
example: 岁月如梭，当年牙牙学语的小明如今已是小学六年级的学生了，令家人感慨万千。
example_en: Time has flown like a weaver's shuttle — little Xiao Ming, who was just learning to talk not so long ago, is now a Primary Six pupil, making the whole family reflect with wonder.

meaning_zh: 将来的时间还长，机会还多，不必着急。
meaning_en: There is still plenty of time ahead; the future is long.
example: 小明这次没有入选校队，老师安慰他来日方长，只要继续努力，机会总会再来。
example_en: Xiao Ming was not selected for the school team this time, but the teacher comforted him that there is still plenty of time ahead — as long as he keeps working hard, another opportunity will come.

meaning_zh: 形容时间或事物极快地消失或过去。
meaning_en: Gone in the blink of an eye; fleeting.
example: 彩虹转瞬即逝，小玲来不及拍照，只好把那美丽的景象深深记在脑海里。
example_en: The rainbow vanished in the blink of an eye — Xiao Ling had no time to take a photo and could only imprint that beautiful sight firmly in her memory.

meaning_zh: 时间不会等待人，要珍惜时间，及时行动。
meaning_en: Time waits for no one; act while you still can.
example: 时不我待，距离PSLE只剩三个月，我们必须抓紧时间复习，不能再拖延了。
example_en: Time waits for no one — with only three months left until the PSLE, we must make the most of every moment and stop procrastinating.

meaning_zh: 形容珍惜每一分每一秒，抓紧时间努力。
meaning_en: To seize every moment; to make every second count.
example: 考试前，同学们只争朝夕，连午休时间也用来温习功课，务求做好充分准备。
example_en: Before the exam, the pupils seized every moment — even using their lunch break to revise, determined to be fully prepared.

meaning_zh: 一旦过去就不会再回来，形容时间、机会的流逝。
meaning_en: Gone and never to return; said of time or opportunity that passes for ever.
example: 小学的时光一去不返，每一天都值得我们好好珍惜，留下美好的回忆。
example_en: The days of primary school are gone and will never return — every single day is worth cherishing and turning into a beautiful memory.

meaning_zh: 事情已经过去，情况也发生了变化。
meaning_en: Things have changed with the passage of time; water under the bridge.
example: 事过境迁，当年和小明闹别扭的同学，如今已经握手言和，成了好朋友。
example_en: Things have changed with the passage of time — the classmate who had a falling-out with Xiao Ming back then has long since made up with him and become a good friend.

meaning_zh: 后来的事物超过先前的，比喻后来者赶上并超越了前者。
meaning_en: The latecomer surpasses those who came before; to overtake with effort.
example: 小峰虽然起步晚，但经过一年的努力，成绩后来居上，超过了班上许多同学。
example_en: Although Xiao Feng started late, after a year of hard work his results overtook many of his classmates — a true case of the latecomer surpassing those who came before.

literal_zh: 住在安全中也想着危险
meaning_zh: 在安全的环境中，也要想到可能发生的危险，做好准备。
meaning_en: In times of peace, prepare for danger; do not be complacent.
example: 老师教导我们要居安思危，成绩好的时候更要努力，不能因为暂时领先而松懈。
example_en: The teacher taught us not to be complacent — even when results are good, we must keep working hard and not relax just because we are temporarily ahead.

meaning_zh: 了解自己，也了解对方，就能在竞争中立于不败之地。
meaning_en: Know yourself and know your opponent; knowledge is the key to victory.
example: 参加辩论赛前，小慧知己知彼，仔细研究了对方的辩题，做好了充分准备。
example_en: Before the debate competition, Xiao Hui knew herself and studied her opponents carefully, preparing thoroughly — knowing both sides is the key to staying undefeated.

meaning_zh: 在灾祸或问题发生之前就加以预防。
meaning_en: To take precautions before trouble arises; prevention is better than cure.
example: 学校定期举行消防演习，就是为了防患未然，让同学们在紧急时刻懂得如何应对。
example_en: The school holds regular fire drills precisely to take precautions before trouble arises — so that pupils know exactly what to do in an emergency.

literal_zh: 根据地方情况制定措施
meaning_zh: 根据当地的实际情况，制定适合的方法或措施。
meaning_en: To adapt measures to local conditions; to tailor one's approach to the situation.
example: 老师因地制宜，为不同程度的同学设计了不同难度的练习题，让每个人都能有所进步。
example_en: The teacher tailored her approach to the situation, designing practice questions of different difficulty levels for pupils at different stages — so that everyone could make progress.

meaning_zh: 从实际情况出发，说真话，做真事，不夸大也不缩小。
meaning_en: To be truthful and realistic; to seek truth from facts.
example: 老师教我们写作文要实事求是，不要为了好看而夸大事实，诚实才是最重要的。
example_en: The teacher taught us that in writing we must be truthful and realistic — never exaggerate for effect, because honesty is what matters most.

literal_zh: 量度自己的力量再去做
meaning_zh: 根据自己的能力去做事，不要超出自己的能力范围。
meaning_en: To act within one's ability; to know one's limits.
example: 老师提醒我们参加课外活动要量力而为，不能因为活动太多而影响了学业。
example_en: The teacher reminded us to know our limits when signing up for CCAs — taking on too much must not be allowed to affect our studies.

meaning_zh: 懂得满足，就能常常感到快乐和满足。
meaning_en: Contentment brings happiness; know when enough is enough.
example: 爷爷常说知足常乐，虽然家里不富裕，但一家人在组屋里其乐融融，生活很充实。
example_en: Grandfather always said that contentment is the key to happiness — even though the family was not wealthy, everyone lived happily together in the HDB flat, and life was full.

meaning_zh: 提前做好准备，就可以避免祸患。
meaning_en: Preparedness averts peril; forewarned is forearmed.
example: 考试前一个月就开始复习，做到有备无患，才不会临时抱佛脚。
example_en: Starting revision a month before the exam — preparedness averts peril, and then you will never have to cram at the last minute.

meaning_zh: 站在别人的立场和角度去思考问题。
meaning_en: To think from another's perspective; to see things from a different point of view.
example: 小丽学会了换位思考，当她和好友发生矛盾时，能冷静地从对方角度去分析问题。
example_en: Xiao Li learnt to see things from another's perspective — whenever she had a conflict with a close friend, she could calmly analyse the situation from the other person's point of view.

meaning_zh: 遵守规则，按照规定行事，不越轨。
meaning_en: To follow the rules; to act in accordance with established norms.
example: 小明在学校一向循规蹈矩，从不违反校规，是老师眼中的好学生。
example_en: Xiao Ming has always followed the rules at school, never breaking school regulations — he is a model student in the eyes of the teachers.

meaning_zh: 形容水面宽阔，烟雾迷蒙，景象壮观。
meaning_en: Vast and misty expanse of water; a magnificent watery scene.
example: 清晨站在滨海湾边，眼前烟波浩渺，远处的城市轮廓在薄雾中若隐若现。
example_en: Standing at Marina Bay in the early morning, a vast and misty expanse of water stretched before us, the city skyline in the distance appearing and disappearing through the haze.

meaning_zh: 形容草木茂盛，碧绿的样子。
meaning_en: Lush and verdant; densely green with thriving vegetation.
example: 东海岸公园的树木郁郁葱葱，沿着小径走进去，感觉像置身于一片小树林中。
example_en: The trees at East Coast Park were lush and verdant — walking along the path into the greenery felt like stepping into a small forest.

meaning_zh: 形容水面在阳光照射下波光闪烁，十分美丽。
meaning_en: Glittering and sparkling waves; sunlight dancing on the water.
example: 清晨，东海岸公园的海面波光粼粼，金色的阳光洒在水面上，美不胜收。
example_en: In the early morning at East Coast Park, the sea surface was glittering and sparkling, golden sunlight scattered across the water — a truly breathtaking sight.

meaning_zh: 形容夏日太阳炽热，像火一样猛烈。
meaning_en: The blazing sun scorching like fire; fierce summer heat.
example: 运动会那天骄阳似火，但同学们仍然精神抖擞地参加各项比赛，没有一个人退缩。
example_en: On Sports Day the sun blazed like fire, but the pupils were still in high spirits competing in every event — not one of them gave up.

meaning_zh: 形容天空布满了乌黑的云，即将下雨。
meaning_en: Dark clouds gathering; the sky covered with black clouds.
example: 下午三点，天空乌云密布，不一会儿就下起了新加坡特有的午后雷阵雨。
example_en: At three in the afternoon, dark clouds gathered across the sky and before long the typical Singapore afternoon thunderstorm began to pour down.

meaning_zh: 形容周围非常安静，没有任何声音。
meaning_en: All is silent; a profound hush falls over everything.
example: 深夜里，组屋区万籁俱寂，只有远处偶尔传来一两声虫鸣，让人心旷神怡。
example_en: Deep in the night, the HDB estate fell into a profound silence — only the occasional chirp of an insect drifting in from afar, soothing the mind and lifting the spirit.

literal_zh: 人的声音像鼎中水沸腾
meaning_zh: 形容人多嘈杂，声音像水开了一样。
meaning_en: A din of voices; a noisy clamour of people.
example: 校庆表演开始前，礼堂里人声鼎沸，同学们兴奋地互相打招呼，热闹非凡。
example_en: Before the school anniversary performance began, the hall was filled with a din of voices as pupils excitedly greeted one another — the atmosphere was electric.

literal_zh: 宁静安详才能达致远方
meaning_zh: 心境宁静，才能实现远大的目标。
meaning_en: Tranquillity of mind leads to far-reaching achievement.
example: 爷爷书房里挂着宁静致远四个字，告诉我们只有静下心来，才能专注地实现梦想。
example_en: Grandfather's study had the four characters 宁静致远 hanging on the wall — a reminder that only by calming one's mind can one focus and achieve great ambitions.

meaning_zh: 形容花朵色彩鲜艳，聚集成堆，十分美丽。
meaning_en: A riot of colour; flowers blooming in magnificent clusters.
example: 学校开放日，走廊两旁摆满了同学们亲手种植的花卉，花团锦簇，令人赏心悦目。
example_en: On the school's Open House, the corridors on both sides were lined with flowers that the pupils had grown themselves — blooming in magnificent clusters, a truly delightful sight.

pinyin: yī shī zú chéng qiān gǔ hèn
literal_zh: 一次失足造成千古遗恨
meaning_zh: 一次严重的错误可能造成终身的遗憾。
meaning_en: One false step can lead to lifelong regret.
example: 老师叮嘱我们考试不要作弊，一失足成千古恨，诚信比分数更重要。
example_en: The teacher warned us never to cheat in exams — one false step can lead to lifelong regret, and integrity matters far more than marks.

pinyin: cōng míng fǎn bèi cōng míng wù
meaning_zh: 自以为聪明，结果反而被自己的小聪明所害。
meaning_en: To be undone by one's own cleverness; too clever by half.
example: 小华以为走捷径可以省时间，结果越做越乱，聪明反被聪明误，浪费了更多时间。
example_en: Xiao Hua thought taking a shortcut would save time, but things got messier and messier — too clever by half, he ended up wasting even more time.

literal_zh: 自己吃下自己种的恶果
meaning_zh: 自己做了坏事，最终要承担自己行为的不良后果。
meaning_en: To reap what one sows; to face the consequences of one's own wrongdoing.
example: 小明考试时偷看答案，结果被发现成绩取消，真是自食恶果。
example_en: Xiao Ming copied answers during the exam and was caught — his results were cancelled, a clear case of reaping what one sows.

literal_zh: 玩弄火焰最终烧到自己
meaning_zh: 比喻做危险的事情，最终害了自己。
meaning_en: To play with fire and get burnt; to bring disaster on oneself.
example: 他屡次逃课，以为不会被发现，结果玩火自焚，被老师叫家长来学校。
example_en: He kept skipping classes, thinking he would never be caught — but he was playing with fire, and in the end the teacher called his parents into school.

meaning_zh: 比喻自己给自己制造麻烦，结果被自己的行为所束缚。
meaning_en: To spin a cocoon around oneself; to be trapped by one's own actions.
example: 他说谎掩盖失误，谎话越说越多，最终作茧自缚，陷入了无法解脱的困境。
example_en: He told lies to cover up his mistake, and the lies kept multiplying — in the end he had spun a cocoon around himself and was trapped in a situation he could not escape.

meaning_zh: 为了小利益而失去了大的利益。
meaning_en: To lose the big picture for small gains; to be penny wise and pound foolish.
example: 小华为了多玩一小时游戏，不肯温习，结果考试失败，真是因小失大。
example_en: Xiao Hua chose to play games for one extra hour instead of revising and ended up failing the exam — a clear case of losing the big picture for a small gain.

meaning_zh: 祸害是从口中说出的话引起的，说话要谨慎。
meaning_en: Trouble comes from careless talk; loose lips sink ships.
example: 小明随口批评了班长，结果引起了误会，险些闹翻，真是祸从口出，说话要三思。
example_en: Xiao Ming made an offhand criticism of the class monitor and triggered a misunderstanding that nearly caused a serious falling-out — trouble comes from careless talk; always think before you speak.

meaning_zh: 贪图享乐，不愿意努力工作或学习。
meaning_en: To love ease and hate work; lazy and pleasure-seeking.
example: 小华好逸恶劳，整天只知道玩游戏，等到考试临近才后悔莫及。
example_en: Xiao Hua loved ease and hated work — he spent every day playing games and only regretted it when exam time arrived.

meaning_zh: 做事不认真，随便应付，草草了事。
meaning_en: To do something in a perfunctory manner; to go through the motions.
example: 小强做家庭作业敷衍了事，字迹潦草，错误百出，被老师要求重做。
example_en: Xiao Qiang was perfunctory with his homework — scrawling messily and making mistake after mistake — and the teacher made him redo it entirely.

meaning_zh: 犯了错误却不改正，继续再犯同样的错误。
meaning_en: To make the same mistake again and again; to repeat one's errors.
example: 老师提醒小华，做错了题不去弄懂，只是更正答案，这样一错再错，永远学不好。
example_en: The teacher warned Xiao Hua that correcting the answer without understanding the mistake means repeating the same errors again and again — you will never truly learn that way.

meaning_zh: 事后非常后悔，但已经来不及了。
meaning_en: To regret too late; to repent when it is already too late.
example: 小明考试前没有认真复习，成绩出来后追悔莫及，暗暗发誓下次一定不再懈怠。
example_en: Xiao Ming had not revised seriously before the exam and was filled with regret when the results came out — he vowed to himself never to be so lax again.

literal_zh: 毛发和骨头都竖立起来
meaning_zh: 形容极度恐惧，毛发竖立，骨头发凉。
meaning_en: To make one's hair stand on end; horrified.
example: 深夜里组屋楼道忽然熄灯，小明独自走在黑暗中，毛骨悚然，脚步越走越快。
example_en: The corridor lights in the HDB block suddenly went out in the middle of the night, and Xiao Ming walking alone in the dark felt his hair stand on end — he quickened his pace with every step.

meaning_zh: 形容极度惊恐，被吓得不知所措。
meaning_en: Frightened out of one's wits; scared to death.
example: 半夜听到楼道里传来奇怪的声响，小丽魂飞魄散，立刻躲进被子里不敢出来。
example_en: Hearing a strange noise in the corridor in the middle of the night, Xiao Li was frightened out of her wits and immediately burrowed under her blanket, too scared to come out.

literal_zh: 一辈子到死都难以忘记
meaning_zh: 一辈子都难以忘记，形容感激或印象极深。
meaning_en: Unforgettable for one's entire life; etched in one's memory for ever.
example: 林老师在小明最低落时给予的鼓励，让他没齿难忘，多年后仍常常提起。
example_en: The encouragement Teacher Lim gave Xiao Ming at his lowest point left an impression he will carry with him for the rest of his life — even years later he still speaks of it often.
